                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Exclusion - global</items>
                            <items id="description">An overall statement of exclusion about all Problems/diagnoses, Family history, Medications, Procedures, Adverse reactions or other clinical items that are either not currently present, or have not been present in the past.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of medication use</items>
                            <items id="description">An overall statement of exclusion about all Problems/diagnoses, Family history, Medications, Procedures, Adverse reactions or other clinical items.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'No previous diagnoses', 'No known family history', 'No known history of adverse reactions', 'Never taken any medications' or 'No known operations or significant procedures'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of problems/diagnoses</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion of all problems or diagnoses at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of family history</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion of all significant health-related problems in relatives or family members of the individual at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of medication use</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about the use of all medications at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of procedures</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about all procedures at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of adverse reactions</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about all adverse reactions at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional comment not covered in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-ACTION.medication.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Immunization statement</items>
                            <items id="description">Any activity related to the planning, scheduling, prescription management, dispensing, administration, cessation and other use of a medication, vaccine, nutritional product or other therapeutic item.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This is not limited to activities performed based on medication orders from clinicians, but could also include for example taking over the counter medication.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Prescription issued</items>
                            <items id="description">A prescription has been issued for the medication.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Prescription dispensed</items>
                            <items id="description">The ordered medication has been dispensed, for example from a pharmacy to the patient.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Medication course commenced</items>
                            <items id="description">The medication has been taken by, or administered to, the patient for the first time. Although in some settings this significant date may be computable as the first of several administrations, in other settings, such as primary care, specific administration dates are not readily available.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Medication reassessed</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual medication has been reassessed, for example whether the medication should still be taken. This is not intended to capture review of the medication list.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Dose administered</items>
                            <items id="description">A single administration of the medication has taken place.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Completed</items>
                            <items id="description">The medication course has been completed as planned.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Prescription supply delayed</items>
                            <items id="description">The prescription has not been dispensed due to a technical or pharamaceutical supply issue.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">On hold</items>
                            <items id="description">The administration of the medication has been suspended until further notice. No further doses should be given until the restart date or conditions have been met. When setting the date/conditions for restart after suspending, a step from ‘Administrations suspended’ and back to 'Medication start date/condition set' should be performed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Prescription re-issued</items>
                            <items id="description">A prescription has been re-issued for an existing medication order.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Prescription re-authorisation pending</items>
                            <items id="description">Issue of the prescription is awaiting re-authorisation by a clinician.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                            <items id="text">Medication course cancelled</items>
                            <items id="description">The planned course of medication has been cancelled prior to any administration.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Medication course postponed</items>
                            <items id="description">The scheduled medication course has been postponed prior to any administration.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Stopped</items>
                            <items id="description">Administration of the medication has been ceased during the period of the intended course.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Medication start date/condition set</items>
                            <items id="description">The time to start the medication, or other starting condition, has been set.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                            <items id="text">Dose administration omitted</items>
                            <items id="description">An administration of the medication has been withheld and not given. There is no expectation that it will be given later, though the next dose (if any) should be administered according to the original order.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                            <items id="text">Immunisation item</items>
                            <items id="description">Name of the medication, vaccine or other therapeutic/prescribable item which was the focus of the activity.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Atenolol 100mg' or 'Tenormin tablets 100mg'. 
It is strongly recommended that the 'Medication item' is coded with a terminology capable of triggering decision support, where possible. The extent of coding may vary from the simple name of the medication item through to structured details about the actual medication pack used. Free text entry should only be used if there is no appropriate terminology available.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Reason</items>
                            <items id="description">Reason that the pathway step for the identified medication was carried out.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Postponed - Patient not avalable at administration time', 'Cancelled - Adverse reaction'. Note: This is not the reason for the medication instruction, but rather the specific reason that a care step was carried out, and will often be used to document some variance from the original order.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0024">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the activity or pathway step not captured in other fields, including details of any variance between the intended action and the action actually performed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Patient was in radiology department', 'Accidental injection into blood vessel during IM administration'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Sequence number</items>
                            <items id="description">The sequence number specific to the pathway step being recorded.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Vaccine sequence number.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0030">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0033">
                            <items id="text">Patient guidance</items>
                            <items id="description">Any guidance, instructions or advice given to the subject of care or personal carer at the time of the pathway step.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Avoid grapefruit' , 'Take at least 2 hours before bedtime', 'Take with food'. May be coded with a terminology when clinically appropriate.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0039">
                            <items id="text">Major change to order</items>
                            <items id="description">A major change to the order was required, resulting in this order being stopped and a replacement order being started.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0041">
                            <items id="text">Minor change to order</items>
                            <items id="description">The medication order has been changed in a manner which does not require a new instruction/order to be issued, according to local clinical rules.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0043">
                            <items id="text">Original scheduled date/time</items>
                            <items id="description">The datetime at which the medication action was scheduled to occur.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Used to compare variance from actual action time where this is not readily calculable from the original instruction.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0044">
                            <items id="text">Dose administration deferred</items>
                            <items id="description">An administration of a dose of the medication has been delayed but is expected to be given as soon as possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0053">
                            <items id="text">Additional details</items>
                            <items id="description">Further structured details of the action, possibly specific to a pathway step.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0085">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0103">
                            <items id="text">Order ID</items>
                            <items id="description">Unique identifier for the medication order.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Comment: This data element allows for multiple occurrences to be defined more explicitly at run-time, if required.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0104">
                            <items id="text">Medication details</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured details about the medication including strength, form and constituent substances.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this SLOT where the detailed description of the dispensed, authorised or administered item needs to be explicitly stated. For example: the form, strength, any diluents or mixture of ingredients.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0106">
                            <items id="text">Prescription re-authorised</items>
                            <items id="description">The original medication order has been re-authorised to allow repeat prescription or dispensing. In some jurisdictions an entirely new order must be issued in these circumstances.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0109">
                            <items id="text">Intended</items>
                            <items id="description">The medication has been recommended but no steps have been taken to initiate prescribing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0131">
                            <items id="text">Amount</items>
                            <items id="description">Specific details about the amount of the medication item.</items>
                            <items id="comment">CLUSTER.dosage is intended to carry details of medication order changes or dose administrations, whilst CLUSTER.medication_supply_amount is intended for details of dispensing-related activity.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0132">
                            <items id="text">Substitution</items>
                            <items id="description">Subsitution action taken by the person administering or dispensing the drug.</items>
                            <items id="comment">In many jurisdictions, substitution of an ordered item as a generic form or with a different brand name, which has been determined as bioequivalent, is allowed at the point of dispense or supply. In other cases substitution is assumed and the clinician has to explicitly request non-substitution.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0133">
                            <items id="text">Substitution reason</items>
                            <items id="description">The reason or justification for the substitution action taken.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: "Generic alternative contains a substance that is not tolerated by the patient." May be coded with a terminology when clinically appropriate</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0138">
                            <items id="text">Substitution performed</items>
                            <items id="description">A medication was substituted which is bioequivalent to that ordered.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0139">
                            <items id="text">Substitution not performed</items>
                            <items id="description">Although allowed by the medication order a bioequvalent medication was not substituted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0140">
                            <items id="text">Administration details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of body site and administration of the medication.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0141">
                            <items id="text">Target site</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured description of the site of administration of the ordered item.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'left upper arm', 'intravenous catheter right hand'. 
Coding of the body site with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0142">
                            <items id="text">Structured body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured description of the site of administration of the medication, vaccine or therapeutic good.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: "On the medial skin surface of the left forearm, from 10 cm to 20 cm distally from the point of the elbow".</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0143">
                            <items id="text">Administration method</items>
                            <items id="description">The technique or device by which the ordered item was, or is to be, administered.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Comment: For example: ' via Z-track injection'; 'via nebuliser'. Coding of the method with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0144">
                            <items id="text">Administration device</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of the medical device used to assist administration of the medication.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0145">
                            <items id="text">Prescription awaiting authorisation</items>
                            <items id="description">Draft prescription has been prepared and is awaiting confirmation from an authorised clinician. May be used where reauthorisations are performed as a batch. This careflow_step may have a status of 'planned' or 'active', reflecting the need to to handle new orders as well as re-authorised orders.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0147">
                            <items id="text">Route</items>
                            <items id="description">The route by which the ordered item was, or is to be, administered into the subject's body.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Comment: For example: 'oral', 'intravenous', or 'topical'. Coding of the route with a terminology is preferred, where possible. Multiple potential routes may be specified.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0148">
                            <items id="text">Medication prepared</items>
                            <items id="description">The medication has been physically prepared.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: preparation of an intravenous mixture.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0149">
                            <items id="text">Double-checked?</items>
                            <items id="description">The pathway step has been checked by a separate individual.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Details of the individual performing the double-check can be carried in the Reference Model element "Participation".</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0150">
                            <items id="text">Prescription cancelled</items>
                            <items id="description">The prescription was cancelled prior to being issued.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0151">
                            <items id="text">Prescription invalid or expired</items>
                            <items id="description">Prescription has been invalidated or has expired without being fulfilled.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0152">
                            <items id="text">Prescription fulfilled</items>
                            <items id="description">The prescription has been fulfilled successfully.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0153">
                            <items id="text">Medication authorised</items>
                            <items id="description">The medication has been formally authorised for use.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0154">
                            <items id="text">Restart date/time</items>
                            <items id="description">The date/time on which the medication course is set to restart, as per the "Administrations suspended" pathway step.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 2017-10-29</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0155">
                            <items id="text">Restart criterion</items>
                            <items id="description">The criterion which triggers the medication course to restart, as per the "Administrations suspended" pathway step.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: "On day 2 after surgery".</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0156">
                            <items id="text">Clinical indication</items>
                            <items id="description">The clinical reason for the medication activity.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Angina' or 'Pain'. Coding of the indication with a terminology is preferred, where possible. This data element allows multiple occurrences. If only an ACTION is used to record a medication indication, this data element can be used without additional consideration. However, if a clinical indication is recorded for both the Medication order INSTRUCTION and this Medication management ACTION, be aware that these indications might not be consistent."</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0157">
                            <items id="text">Medication double checked</items>
                            <items id="description">The prepared medication has been double checked.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0158">
                            <items id="text">Dose administration aborted</items>
                            <items id="description">An administration of a dose of the medication was started but was subsequently aborted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Absence of information</items>
                            <items id="description">Statement that specified health information is not available for inclusion in the health record or extract at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Absence statement</items>
                            <items id="description">Positive statement that no information is available.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: "No information available about adverse reactions"; No information available about problems or diagnoses"; "No information available about previous procedures performed"; or "No information available about medications used".</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date at which the absence was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Reason for absence</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the reason why there is no information available.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: patient is unconscious or refuses to provide information. Coding the reason with a terminology is desirable, if possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                        </interval>
                    </cardinality>

                                                        <archetype_id>
                                                            <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.specimen.v1</value>
                                                        </archetype_id>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                                            <items id="text">Specimen</items>
                                                            <items id="description">A physical sample collected from, or related to, an individual for the purpose of investigation, examination or analysis.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: Tissue or body fluid.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                                            <items id="text">Laboratory specimen identifier</items>
                                                            <items id="description">A unique identifier of the specimen, normally assigned by the laboratory.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">Sometimes called the Accession Identifier. Specimen containers, for example vacuum vials or tissue cassettes, have their own identitiers which may be recorded in the 'Container identifier' element in the 'Specimen container' archetype.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                                            <items id="text">Parent specimen identifier</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Unique identifier of the parent specimen, where the specimen is split into sub-samples.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: A specific histology slide specimen can have a specific paraffin wax block as its parent specimen.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                                            <items id="text">Hazard warning</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Identified health risk or biohazard to the collector or laboratory staff due to exposure to, or contact with, the specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Hepatitis B'; "nerve agent'. Coding of the 'Hazard warning' with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                                            <items id="text">Method</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The method of collection used.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: venepuncture, biopsy, resection. Coding of the collection method with a terminology is preferred, where possible. If the collection method is included in the 'Specimen type' via precoordinated codes, this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                                                            <items id="text">Sampling context</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The context in which the specimen is collected.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: 'fasting'; 'full bladder'; 'sterile field'; or special instructions on the handling or immediate processing of the sample, such as 'centrifuge on receipt'. This data element also be used to document any known deviations from collection or handling instructions, for example that the patient had not fasted, even when this had been originally requested. Coding of the 'Sampling context' with a terminology is preferred, where possible.

If this archetype is used within an INSTRUCTION archetype, the context will reflect the intended or desired conditions at sample collection. If this archetype is used for representing an ACTION archetype pathway step which correlates with the collection being performed or completed, the context will be reflecting the conditions that existed at sample collection and this may also be copied to the 'Confounding factors' element of the OBSERVATION.laboratory_test_result archetype.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                                                            <items id="text">Structured source site</items>
                                                            <items id="description">A structured description of the area of the body from where the specimen is collected.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">Utilise the more detailed archetypes to describe structured or more complex anatomical sites, or to support recording the source site at run-time by the application. If the body site has been fully identified in the 'Source site' data element, this SLOT becomes redundant.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                                                            <items id="text">Collection date/time</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The date and time that collection has been ordered to take place or has taken place.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">This datetime will be captured primarily in the INSTRUCTION timing, ACTION time or OBSERVATION times. However, as this is a critical piece of information, it can be useful to also associate it directly with the specimen itself.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                                                            <items id="text">Physical properties</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Physical dimensions, mass or non-measurable properties of the specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: Volume, mass, circumference, colour, smell, turbidity. This element can be used to specify the properties of the specimen to be collected, in the context of an INSTRUCTION archetype, or the properties of the specimen which was collected, in the context of an ACTION or OBSERVATION archetype. For example, an INSTRUCTION may request the collection of 20 ml of blood, while the corresponding ACTION records that only 15 ml was collected.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0029">
                                                            <items id="text">Specimen type</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The type of specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: Venous blood, bacterial culture, cytology, or tissue sample. Coding of the specimen type with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0034">
                                                            <items id="text">Date/time received</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The date and time that the sample was received at the laboratory.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0041">
                                                            <items id="text">Adequacy for testing</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Information about whether the specimen was adequate for testing.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">This element is intended to be used to record a judgement about whether or not the specimen quality was good enough for testing, and whether or not testing has been carried out. Coding of the specimen quality issues with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0042">
                                                            <items id="text">Specimen quality issue</items>
                                                            <items id="description">A specific quality issue with a specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">Coding of the specimen quality issues with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0045">
                                                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the specimen not captured in other fields.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0052">
                                                            <items id="text">Haemolysed</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen was haemolysed.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0053">
                                                            <items id="text">Lipaemic</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen was lipaemic.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0054">
                                                            <items id="text">Incorrect additive</items>
                                                            <items id="description">An incorrect additive such as a transport medium or preservative was added to the specimen.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                                                            <items id="text">Insufficient amount</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The available amount of specimen was insufficient to undertake the examination.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                                                            <items id="text">Satisfactory</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen is of sufficient quality to allow reporting.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0063">
                                                            <items id="text">Unsatisfactory - analysed</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen is unsatisfactory but has been analysed.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                                                            <items id="text">Unsatisfactory - not analysed</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen is unsatisfactory and has not been analysed.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0067">
                                                            <items id="text">Collection setting</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Identification of the physical setting in which the specimen is collected.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: laboratory outpatient clinic, at home, or hospital ward. This specifies the type of specimen collection location within the healthcare environment. The specimen collection setting may provide additional information relevant to the analysis of the result.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0068">
                                                            <items id="text">Processing details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Structured details about preparation or processing of the specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: Staining or fixation.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0070">
                                                            <items id="text">Specimen collector identifier</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Identifier of the person or organisation responsible for collecting the specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">This element is intended to be used where the specimen has already been collected and the actual collector is known.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0071">
                                                            <items id="text">Specimen collector details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The person or organisation responsible for collecting the specimen.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0079">
                                                            <items id="text">Collection description</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the collection of the specimen.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0080">
                                                            <items id="text">Number of containers</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The total number of physical units holding this specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: Number of buckets, jars, vials, tubes, blocks, slides or grids.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0083">
                                                            <items id="text">Additional details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: details about needle biopsies in prostate cancer, where both the request and reporting about the specimen are detailed and specific.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0085">
                                                            <items id="text">Container details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Details about containers used.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0087">
                                                            <items id="text">Body site</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Identification of the body site or other location from where the specimen is collected.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: 'wound on left calf', 'IV cannula right arm', 'right kidney'.
Coding of the name of the source site with a terminology is preferred, where possible. Use this data element to record precoordinated source sites. If the requirements for recording the source site are determined at run-time by the application or require more complex modelling such as relative locations then use the 'Structured source site' SLOT in this archetype. If the source site is included in the 'Specimen type' via precoordinated codes, this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0088">
                                                            <items id="text">External identifier</items>
                                                            <items id="description">A unique identifier of the specimen, assigned by a party external to the laboratory.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Requester ID', 'Archive ID, 'Biobank ID'.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0089">
                                                            <items id="text">Icteric</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen was icteric.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0090">
                                                            <items id="text">Handling error</items>
                                                            <items id="description">An error arose when handling the specimen. For example: Incorrect storage, broken container.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0091">
                                                            <items id="text">Age</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen was too old to analyse or to analyse accurately.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0092">
                                                            <items id="text">Technical failure</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen could not be analysed for technical reasons.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0093">
                                                            <items id="text">Transport details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Structured details about transport of the specimen.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0094">
                                                            <items id="text">Clotted</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen was clotted.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0095">
                                                            <items id="text">Incorrectly labelled</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The specimen was labelled incorrectly.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0096">
                                                            <items id="text">Digital representation</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Structured details about a digital representation of the specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example the scanned image of a histopathology slide.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0097">
                                                            <items id="text">Specimen description</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the specimen being examined.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: the color and viscosity of a liquid; the shape of a solid tissue; or a description of tissue fragments.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0098">
                                                            <items id="text">Specimen label</items>
                                                            <items id="description">A name or label describing the specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">Usually only relevant for anatomical pathology samples. For example: 'Sigmoid resection with a piece of bladder wall' or 'Aggregate of multiple skin biopsy fragments'.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0099">
                                                            <items id="text">Number of fragments</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The number of tissue fragments comprising the specimen.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">Usually only relevant when a number of tissue fragments are being examined and reported on as a single specimen.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0100">
                                                            <items id="text">Storage details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Structured details about the storage of the specimen.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                    </children>

                                                        <archetype_id>
                                                            <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.laboratory_test_analyte.v1</value>
                                                        </archetype_id>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                                            <items id="text">Laboratory analyte result</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The result of a laboratory test for a single analyte value.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                                            <items id="text">Analyte result</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The value of the analyte result.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example '7.3 mmol/l', 'Raised'. The 'Any' data type will need to be constrained to an appropriate data type in a specialisation, a template or at run-time to reflect the actual analyte result. The Quantity data type has reference model attributes that include flags for normal/abnormal, reference ranges and approximations - see https://specifications.openehr.org/releases/RM/latest/data_types.html#_dv_quantity_class for more details.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the analyte result, not captured in other fields.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                                                            <items id="text">Reference range guidance</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional advice on the applicability of the reference range to this result or may carry text or coded textual guidance as to whether the result is within the normal range.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example, 'within normal limits for age and sex'.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                                            <items id="text">Result status</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The status of the analyte result value.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">The values have been specifically chosen to match those in the HL7 FHIR Diagnostic report, historically derived from HL7v2 practice. Other local codes/terms can be used via the Text 'choice'.

This element allows multiple occurrences to support use cases where more than one type of status need to be implemented.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                                                            <items id="text">Result status time</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The date and time that the analyte result was issued for the recorded ‘Result status’.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                                                            <items id="text">Analyte result detail</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Further detail regarding an individual result.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                                                            <items id="text">Registered</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The existence of the test is registered in the Laboratory Information System, but there is nothing yet available.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                                                            <items id="text">Partial</items>
                                                            <items id="description">This is a partial (e.g. initial, interim or preliminary) Test Result: data in the Test Result may be incomplete or unverified.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                                                            <items id="text">Preliminary</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Verified early results are available, but not all results are final. This is a sub-category of 'Partial'.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                                                            <items id="text">Final</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The Test result is complete and verified by an authorised person.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                                                            <items id="text">Corrected</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The result has been modified subsequent to being Final, and is complete and verified by an authorised person. This is a sub-category of 'Amended'.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                                                            <items id="text">Amended</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The result has been modified subsequent to being Final, and is complete and verified by an authorised person, and result data has been changed.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                                                            <items id="text">Appended</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Subsequent to being final, the report has been modified by adding new content. The existing content is unchanged. This is a sub-category of 'Amended'.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                                                            <items id="text">Entered in error</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The Test Result has been withdrawn following previous Final release.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                                                            <items id="text">Cancelled</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The result is unavailable because the test was not started or not completed (also sometimes called 'aborted').</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0024">
                                                            <items id="text">Analyte name</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The name of the analyte result.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">The value for this element is normally supplied in a specialisation, in a template or at run-time to reflect the actual analyte. For example: 'Serum sodium', 'Haemoglobin'. Coding with an external terminology is strongly recommended, such as LOINC, NPU, SNOMED CT, or local lab terminologies.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                                                            <items id="text">Validation time</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The date and time that the analyte result was validated in the laboratory by a healthcare practitioner.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">In many jurisdictions the 'Result status' is assumed to include medical validation i.e. a 'final' result will be assumed to be medically validated, but in others this will be recorded and reported separately using this data element.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                                                            <items id="text">Specimen</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Identification of the specimen used for the analyte result.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">In some situations, a single Laboratory test result archetype will contain multiple Specimen archetypes and multiple Analyte result archetypes. In these situations, this 'Specimen' data element is needed to be able to connect the results with the correct specimens.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                                                            <items id="text">Analyte result sequence</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The intended position of this analyte result within the overall sequence of analyte results.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: '1' '2', '3'. Where multiple analyte results are reported, the 'Analyte result sequence' makes the order in which they were reported explicit.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0028">
                                                            <items id="text">Test method</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Description about the method used to perform the test on this analyte only.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">If the test method applies to an entire panel, the test method can be captured using the 'Test method' data element within the OBSERVATION.laboratory_test_result</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                    </children>

                                                        <archetype_id>
                                                            <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.media_file.v1</value>
                                                        </archetype_id>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                                            <items id="text">Media file</items>
                                                            <items id="description">A media file that is acquired or used as part of the healthcare process, and associated metadata.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                                            <items id="text">Content</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Digital representation of the media file.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">If the file is stored locally, the actual content will be captured and stored using the Multimedia data type. For example: RTF or PDF for a document; JPG for an image; MP4 for a video; or WAV for an audio file. Full list of available media types: https://specifications.openehr.org/releases/TERM/latest/SupportTerminology.html#_media_types.  If the file is stored at a location outside of the health record, the detailed path to the file is captured using the URI attribute in the Multimedia data type. The Multimedia data type has many RM attributes such as the size of file and URI to an external source - see https://specifications.openehr.org/releases/RM/latest/data_types.html#_dv_multimedia_class.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                                            <items id="text">Content name</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Descriptive name or title for the media file.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">A description of the content of the media file. Use an agreed standardised naming convention, if available. For example: "Right hand burn #1" or "Cardiology video consultation on May 2".</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                                                            <items id="text">Created</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The date/time, partial date or period when the media file was generated or authored.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the media file.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the media file not captured in other fields.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                                                            <items id="text">Identifier</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Unique ID for the media file.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                                                            <items id="text">Source device</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Details about the device used to generate or author the media file.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: the camera used to capture an image.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                                                            <items id="text">Creator</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Details about the individual or organisation who generated or authored the media file.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                                                            <items id="text">Additional details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the media file.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                    </children>

                                                        </interval>
