                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-OBSERVATION.laboratory_test_result.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Laboratory test result</items>
                            <items id="description">The result, including findings and the laboratory's interpretation, of an investigation performed on specimens collected from an individual or related to that individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Event Series</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>
                            <items id="description">Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Test name</items>
                            <items id="description">Name of the laboratory investigation performed on the specimen(s).</items>
                            <items id="comment">A test result may be for a single analyte, or a group of items, including panel tests. It is strongly recommended that 'Test name' be coded with a terminology, for example LOINC or SNOMED CT. For example: 'Glucose', 'Urea and Electrolytes', 'Swab', 'Cortisol (am)', 'Potassium in perspiration' or 'Melanoma histopathology'. The name may sometimes include specimen type and patient state, for example 'Fasting blood glucose' or include other information, as 'Potassium (PNA blood gas)'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Receiving laboratory</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of the laboratory which received the request and has overall responsibility to manage reporting of the test, even if other labs perform specific aspects.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This slot is intended to carry details of the laboratory which received the request and has overall responsibility to manage reporting of the test, even if other labs perform specific aspects.

The receiving laboratory may either perform the test or refer it to another laboratory. Where a different laboratory is responsible for performing the testing on specific analytes, it would be expected that these details would be carried in the 'Analyte result detail' SLOT within the CLUSTER.laboratory_test_analyte archetype.

</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0035">
                            <items id="text">Distribution list</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of additional clinicians or organisations who require a copy of the test result.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The 'Distribution list' is for information-only, and that the primary recipient of the report is the person intended to act on the information.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0037">
                            <items id="text">Partial</items>
                            <items id="description">This is a partial (e.g. initial, interim or preliminary) Test Result: data in the Test Result may be incomplete or unverified.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0038">
                            <items id="text">Final</items>
                            <items id="description">The Test result is complete and verified by an authorised person.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0040">
                            <items id="text">Amended</items>
                            <items id="description">The result has been modified subsequent to being Final, and is complete and verified by the responsible pathologist, and result data has been changed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0057">
                            <items id="text">Interpretation</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description of the key findings.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Pattern suggests significant renal impairment'. The content of the conclusion will vary, depending on the investigation performed. This conclusion should be aligned with the coded 'Test diagnosis'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Requester order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">The local identifier assigned by the requesting clinical system.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Equivalent to the HL7 Placer Order Identifier.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0063">
                            <items id="text">Receiver order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">The local identifier assigned to the test order by the order filler, usually by the Laboratory Information System (LIS).</items>
                            <items id="comment">Assigning an identifier to a request by the Laboratory lnformation System (LIS) enables tracking progress of the request and enables linking results to requests. It also provides a reference to assist with enquiries and it is usually equivalent to the HL7 Filler Order Identifier.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0065">
                            <items id="text">Specimen detail</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the physical substance that has been analysed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">If the specimen type is sufficiently specified with a code in the Test name, then this additional data is not required. Linking results to specific specimens may be recorded using 'Specimen identifier' elements in both the CLUSTER.specimen and the various results CLUSTER archetypes.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0068">
                            <items id="text">Laboratory internal identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">A local identifier assigned by the receiving Laboratory Information System (LIS) to track the test process.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This identifier is an internal tracking number assigned by the LIS, and it not intended to be the name of the test.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0073">
                            <items id="text">Overall test status</items>
                            <items id="description">The status of the laboratory test result as a whole.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The values have been specifically chosen to match those in the HL7 FHIR Diagnostic report, historically derived from HL7v2 practice. Other local codes/terms can be used via the Text 'choice'.

This element is multiple occurrence to cater for the use cases where statuses for different aspects of the result have been split into several elements.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0074">
                            <items id="text">Cancelled</items>
                            <items id="description">The result is unavailable because the test was not started or not completed (also sometimes called 'aborted').</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0075">
                            <items id="text">Overall test status timestamp</items>
                            <items id="description">The date and/or time that ‘Overall test status’ was issued.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0077">
                            <items id="text">Diagnostic service category</items>
                            <items id="description">The diagnostic service or discipline that is responsible for the laboratory test result.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This is intended to be a general categorisation and not to capture the organisational name of the laboratory. For example: anatomical pathology, immunology and transfusion medicine, medical microbiology, clinical pharmacology, medical genetics, medical biochemistry. Alternatively more granular sub categories or sub disciplines, such as endocrinology, haematology, and allergology services, may be used. This may assist clinicians in filtering between categories of results. Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0090">
                            <items id="text">Requester</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of the clinician or organisation requesting the laboratory test result.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0094">
                            <items id="text">Test request details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the test request.</items>
                            <items id="comment">In most situations there is one test request and a single corresponding test result, however this repeating cluster allows for the situation where there may be multiple test requests reported using a single test result.

As an example: 'a clinician asks for blood glucose in one request and Urea/electrolytes in a second request, but the lab analyser does both and the lab wishes to report these together'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0097">
                            <items id="text">Test result</items>
                            <items id="description">Results of the test performed on the specimen(s).</items>
                            <items id="comment">This SLOT may carry an individual analyte, a group, panel or battery of multiple analytes, or a more complex and specific structure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0098">
                            <items id="text">Test diagnosis</items>
                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description that represents the clinical meaning and significance of the laboratory test result.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Severe hepatic impairment', 'Salmonella contamination'. Coding of the diagnosis with a terminology is strongly recommended, where possible. This diagnosis should be aligned with the narrative in the 'Conclusion'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0100">
                            <items id="text">Clinical information provided</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of clinical information available at the time of interpretation of results.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element may include a link to the original clinical information provided in the test request.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0101">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the test result not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0106">
                            <items id="text">Original test requested name</items>
                            <items id="description">Name of the original laboratory test requested.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element is to be used when the test requested differs from the test actually performed by the laboratory.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0107">
                            <items id="text">Registered</items>
                            <items id="description">The existence of the test is registered in the Laboratory Information System, but there is nothing yet available.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0110">
                            <items id="text">Testing details</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured details about the method of analysis, device or interpretation used.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'details of ELISA/nephelometry'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0111">
                            <items id="text">Point-of-care test</items>
                            <items id="description">This indicates whether the test was performed directly at Point-of-Care (POCT) as opposed to a formal result from a laboratory or other service delivery organisation.</items>
                            <items id="comment">True if the test was performed directly at Point-of-Care (POCT).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0112">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0113">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Issues or circumstances that impact on the accurate interpretation of the measurement or test result.</items>
                            <items id="comment">'Confounding factors' should be reserved for uncontrolled/unplanned issues of patient state/physiology that might affect interpretation, for example 'recent exercise' or 'recent tobacco smoking'.

Known or required preconditions, such as 'fasting' should be carried in the 'Sampling conditions' element within the CLUSTER.specimen archetype . In some cases preconditions are captured as part of the test name, for example 'Fasting blood glucose'.

Known issues with specimen collection or handling, such as 'prolonged use of tourniquet' or 'sample haemolysed', should be carried in the 'Specimen quality' elements within CLUSTER.specimen archetype.

Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0114">
                            <items id="text">Structured confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of issues or circumstances that impact on the accurate interpretation of the measurement or test result.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Last Normal Menstrual Period (LNMP).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0115">
                            <items id="text">Corrected</items>
                            <items id="description">The result has been modified subsequent to being Final, and is complete and verified by the responsible pathologist. This is a sub-category of 'Amended'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0116">
                            <items id="text">Entered in error</items>
                            <items id="description">The Test Result has been withdrawn following previous Final release.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0117">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0118">
                            <items id="text">Multimedia representation</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital image, video or diagram representing the test result.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Multiple formats are allowed but they should represent equivalent clinical content.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0119">
                            <items id="text">Appended</items>
                            <items id="description">Subsequent to being final, the report has been modified by adding new content. The existing content is unchanged. This is a sub-category of 'Amended'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0120">
                            <items id="text">Preliminary</items>
                            <items id="description">Verified early results are available, but not all results are final. This is a sub-category of 'Partial'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0121">
                            <items id="text">Test method</items>
                            <items id="description">Description about the method used to perform the test.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0122">
                            <items id="text">Structured test diagnosis</items>
                            <items id="description">A structured or complex diagnosis for the laboratory test.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Anatomical pathology diagnoses consisting of several different axes such as morphology, etiology and function.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                                                        <archetype_id>
                                                            <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.imaging_exam.v1</value>
                                                        </archetype_id>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                                            <items id="text">Imaging examination of a body structure</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Findings on radiological examination of a specified body structure or region.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                                            <items id="text">Body structure</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Identification of the body structure or region examined.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Liver', 'Right ankle' or 'Lymph node group'. Coding of the body structure with an appropriate terminology, such as SNOMED CT, is recommended. If the body structure has been fully identified in this 'Body structure' element, then the 'Body site' data element and 'Structured body site' SLOT become redundant.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                                            <items id="text">Body site</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Identification of the area of the body under examination.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: a lymph node group (body structure) which found in the 'Right axilla'. Coding of 'Body site' with a terminology, such as SNOMED CT, is desirable. If the body site has been fully identified in the 'Body structure' data element, this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                                            <items id="text">Structured body site</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Structured details about the area of the body under examination.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: details about a relative anatomical location, such as '2cm distal to the appendix'. If the body site has been fully identified in the 'Body structure' or 'Body site' data elements, then this SLOT becomes redundant.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                                                            <items id="text">Imaging findings</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Narrative description of the imaging findings observed during this examination.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                                            <items id="text">Additional details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about imaging findings for the identified body structure or region, or findings of related structures.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                                                            <items id="text">Impression</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description that represents the clinical meaning and significance of all imaging findings for the identified body structure.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">Also referred to as 'Interpretation' or 'Opinion'. For example: 'No abnormality detected' or 'Free fluid present'. Coding of the 'Impression' with a terminology is permitted, if useful. Multiple occurrences allow for more than one coded impression. A narrative description may include a comparison with previous studies of the identified body structure, if appropriate.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the imaging findings of the body structure, not captured in other fields.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                    </children>

                                                        <archetype_id>
                                                            <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.media_file.v1</value>
                                                        </archetype_id>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                                            <items id="text">Media file</items>
                                                            <items id="description">A media file that is acquired or used as part of the healthcare process, and associated metadata.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                                            <items id="text">Content</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Digital representation of the media file.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">If the file is stored locally, the actual content will be captured and stored using the Multimedia data type. For example: RTF or PDF for a document; JPG for an image; MP4 for a video; or WAV for an audio file. Full list of available media types: https://specifications.openehr.org/releases/TERM/latest/SupportTerminology.html#_media_types.  If the file is stored at a location outside of the health record, the detailed path to the file is captured using the URI attribute in the Multimedia data type. The Multimedia data type has many RM attributes such as the size of file and URI to an external source - see https://specifications.openehr.org/releases/RM/latest/data_types.html#_dv_multimedia_class.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                                            <items id="text">Content name</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Descriptive name or title for the media file.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">A description of the content of the media file. Use an agreed standardised naming convention, if available. For example: "Right hand burn #1" or "Cardiology video consultation on May 2".</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                                                            <items id="text">Created</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The date/time, partial date or period when the media file was generated or authored.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the media file.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the media file not captured in other fields.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                                                            <items id="text">Identifier</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Unique ID for the media file.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                                                            <items id="text">Source device</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Details about the device used to generate or author the media file.</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">For example: the camera used to capture an image.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                                                            <items id="text">Creator</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Details about the individual or organisation who generated or authored the media file.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                                                            <items id="text">Additional details</items>
                                                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the media file.</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-OBSERVATION.imaging_exam_result.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Imaging examination result</items>
                            <items id="description">The result of an imaging examination performed on an individual, using radiological techniques.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Event Series</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>
                            <items id="description">Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Study name</items>
                            <items id="description">The name of the imaging examination performed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'CT abdomen'; 'Doppler ultrasound of renal artery' or 'Plain chest xray'. Coding with a terminology is strongly recommended, preferably a term specifying both modality and anatomical location, where possible. Examples of candidate terminologies are the LOINC Imaging Document Codes, RadLex Playbook or SNOMED-CT. Equivalent to DiagnosticReport.code in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Structured target body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured detail about the body site or region targetted for imaging.</items>
                            <items id="comment">If the body site is specified within the text or code for 'Study name', then this field may be redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Imaging findings</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description or overview of all clinical findings.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Clinical summary</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description of relevant clinical history that provides context for the examination and interpretation of results.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Alternatively this data element can include an RM link to clinical information provided in the original examination request or located elsewhere within the health record.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                            <items id="text">Imaging diagnosis</items>
                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description representing the likely condition or diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element has multiple occurrences to allow for more than one differential diagnosis. Coding with a terminology such as SNOMED CT is preferred, where possible. This data element should be considered as mutually exclusive to "Imaging differential diagnosis", ie only either "Imaging diagnosis" or "Imaging differential diagnosis" in each instance of the archetype "Imaging examination result." Equivalent to DiagnosticReport.conclusionCode in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Overall impression</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative concise, clinically relevant interpretation of all imaging findings, and include a comparison with previous studies where appropriate.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Also referred to as 'Opinion' or 'Conclusion'. Equivalent to DiagnosticReport.conclusion in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the examination not captured in other fields.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: a note that the film was given to the patient. Equivalent to DiagnosticReport.note in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                            <items id="text">Imaging service</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the service, organisation or individual carrying out the imaging examination.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                            <items id="text">Examination request details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about a single imaging examination requested.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0028">
                            <items id="text">Requester order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">Unique identifier for the imaging examination order assigned by the requester.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Equivalent to the HL7 Placer Order Identifier.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0029">
                            <items id="text">Examination requested name</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of imaging examination requested.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This can be useful in situations where the examination requested differs from the examination performed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0030">
                            <items id="text">Requester</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the clinician and/or organisation requesting the imaging examination.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Equivalent to DICOM Referring Physician's Name (0008,0090)</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0031">
                            <items id="text">Receiver order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">Unique identifier for the imaging examination order assigned by the radiology service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Equivalent to the HL7 Filler Order Identifier or DICOM Accession number attribute (0008,0050).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0041">
                            <items id="text">Structured technique/procedure</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured details of technical and clinical aspects of capturing the image/s.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0044">
                            <items id="text">Structured imaging findings</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured details about the imaging examination findings targeting a specific structure or region.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0045">
                            <items id="text">Report representation</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital representation of the examination result.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Multiple formats are allowed but they should represent equivalent clinical content. For example a pdf version of the report. Equivalent to DiagnosticReport.presentedForm in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0046">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to extend the model with local content or to align with other reference models or formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements; or additional metadata to align with FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0047">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0048">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description of factors, not recorded elsewhere, that may influence the examination findings and/or result.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0049">
                            <items id="text">Technique summary</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the radiological procedure used to capture the study.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this data element to describe a summary of technique, including technical limitations or deviation from the normal technical procedures. For example: '5 mm axial images from the lung bases through the pubic symphysis were acquired following the administration of intravenous and oral contrast. Coronal and Sagittal reformatted images were constructed from the source data.'</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                            <items id="text">Target body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the simple body site or region targetted for imaging.</items>
                            <items id="comment">If the body site is specified within the text or code for 'Study name', then this field may be redundant. Coding of 'Target body site' with a terminology, such as SNOMED CT, is desirable. Equivalent to ImagingStudy.series.bodySite in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0056">
                            <items id="text">Comparison findings</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the comparison of this examination with previous similar studies.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Also use this data element to indicate if no comparison images and/or reports are available. Details about the images used for comparison can be recorded using the 'Comparison study details' CLUSTER, within Protocol.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0057">
                            <items id="text">Imaging quality description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the quality of the examination.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the nature of any limitations and their impact on interpretation; recommendation that the examination should be repeated; or whether an alternative method of assessment might be more appropriate.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0058">
                            <items id="text">Imaging differential diagnosis</items>
                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description representing a possible condition or diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element has multiple occurrences to allow for more than one differential diagnosis. Coding with a terminology such as SNOMED CT is preferred, where possible. This data element should be considered as mutually exclusive to "Imaging diagnosis", ie only either "Imaging diagnosis" or "Imaging differential diagnosis" in each instance of the archetype "Imaging examination result."</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0059">
                            <items id="text">Recommendation</items>
                            <items id="description">Suggestion for further imaging, investigations and/or referral, and associated rationale.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element has 0..* occurrences to allow for more than one recommendation. Formal orders for additional imaging examination, investigation should be recorded using an INSTRUCTION archetype, such as INSTRUCTION.service_request.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0061">
                            <items id="text">Clinical indication</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the reason the examination was originally requested.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Also known as 'Clinical question' or 'Clinical query'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Procedure summary</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the clinical procedure or other clinical considerations used to capture the study.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this data element to describe a summary of the clinical procedure, patient positioning, interventions such as Valsalva manoeuvre, and administration of contrast agent, radiopharmaceuticals and/or treatments as part of the imaging study. For example: '80 ml of Isovue-370 was administered intravenously using a bolus-tracking technique centered on the main pulmonary artery'. It can also be used to record an overview of any issues, complications or adverse reactions encountered. </items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0063">
                            <items id="text">Imaging quality</items>
                            <items id="description">Assessment about the quality of the examination.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the imaging quality with a value set is recommended. For example: 'excellent, good, fair, poor, and non-diagnostic'; or 'adequate, suboptimal and inadequate'.
</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0065">
                            <items id="text">Series details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about a series included in this report.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0067">
                            <items id="text">Comparison series details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about an imaging series being compared to the reported study.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0069">
                            <items id="text">Device</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about imaging device/s used to capture the study.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0070">
                            <items id="text">Study date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date/time when the imaging started.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Equivalent to ImagingStudy.started in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0071">
                            <items id="text">Status timestamp</items>
                            <items id="description">The date and/or time that ‘Overall result status’ was assigned.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0072">
                            <items id="text">Overall result status</items>
                            <items id="description">The status of the imaging examination result as a whole.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The values have been aligned with the FHIR Diagnostic report status, with the exception of 'Entered in error' which is relevant only to a messaging-oriented paradigm. Other local codes/terms can be used via the Text 'choice'. In most use cases, only one status is applicable at any time, however this data element allows multiple occurrences for the use cases where there are a variety of types of status that need to be specified, usually within a template. Equivalent to DiagnosticReport.status in FHIR. </items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0073">
                            <items id="text">Registered</items>
                            <items id="description">The existence of the study is registered, but no result is yet available.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0074">
                            <items id="text">Partial</items>
                            <items id="description">This is a partial (e.g. initial, interim or preliminary) result but data may be incomplete or unverified.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0075">
                            <items id="text">Preliminary</items>
                            <items id="description">Verified early results are available, but not all results are final. This is a sub-category of 'Partial'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0076">
                            <items id="text">Final</items>
                            <items id="description">The result is complete and verified by an authorised person.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0077">
                            <items id="text">Amended</items>
                            <items id="description">Subsequent to being final, the result has been modified.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0078">
                            <items id="text">Corrected</items>
                            <items id="description">Subsequent to being final, the result has been modified to correct an error. This is a sub-category of 'Amended'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0079">
                            <items id="text">Appended</items>
                            <items id="description">Subsequent to being final, the result has been modified by adding new content. The existing content is unchanged. This is a sub-category of 'Amended'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0080">
                            <items id="text">Cancelled</items>
                            <items id="description">The result is unavailable because the test was not started or not completed (also sometimes called 'aborted').</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0083">
                            <items id="text">Distribution list</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of additional clinicians or organisations who require a copy of the examination result.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The 'Distribution list' is for information-only, and that the primary recipient of the report is the person intended to act on the information.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0087">
                            <items id="text">Technique</items>
                            <items id="description">Name of the radiological procedure used to capture the study.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Technique' with a terminology is permitted. Multiple occurrences allow for identification of one or more techniques.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0088">
                            <items id="text">Procedure</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the clinical procedure or management used to capture the study.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0090">
                            <items id="text">Unknown</items>
                            <items id="description">The status of the result is not known.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0091">
                            <items id="text">Modality</items>
                            <items id="description">The type of imaging device, process or method that originally acquired or produced the data used to create the images in the study.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'XRay', 'CT scan', 'ultrasound', 'MRI', 'bone densitometry', 'tomography', 'mammography', 'nuclear medicine', 'PET', 'angiography', and 'fluoroscopy'. This data element may be redundant if the modality is clearly represented in the 'Study name'. Alternatively it may also be possible to autopopulate the 'Modality' from a coded 'Study name'. Coding of the 'Modality' with a value set is recommended, such as the DICOM CID 29 Acquisition Modality value set. Equivalent to ImagingStudy.modality in FHIR and Modality (0008, 0060) in DICOM.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0092">
                            <items id="text">Study instance identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">Unique identifier for the imaging study assigned by the imaging device.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Equivalent to Study Instance UID (0020,000D) in DICOM and ImagingStudy.identifier in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0093">
                            <items id="text">Comparison study details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about images from a prior study used for comparison to the reported study.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0094">
                            <items id="text">Study name</items>
                            <items id="description">The name of the comparison imaging examination performed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0095">
                            <items id="text">Study identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">Unique identifier for the comparison imaging study.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0096">
                            <items id="text">Study date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date and time the comparison examination started.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0097">
                            <items id="text">Study end point</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital location of the comparison imaging content and metadata.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This location identifies the network service providing access (for storage, viewing, querying, or retrieval) for the study. Equivalent to ImagingStudy.endpoint in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0098">
                            <items id="text">Study end point</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital location of the imaging content and metadata.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This location identifies the network service providing access (for storage, viewing, querying, or retrieval) for the study. Equivalent to ImagingStudy.endpoint in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0099">
                            <items id="text">Study status</items>
                            <items id="description">The current state of the imaging study.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Equivalent to ImagingStudy.status in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0100">
                            <items id="text">Registered</items>
                            <items id="description">The existence of the imaging study is registered, but there is nothing yet available.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0101">
                            <items id="text">Available</items>
                            <items id="description">At least one instance has been associated with this imaging study.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0102">
                            <items id="text">Cancelled</items>
                            <items id="description">The imaging study is unavailable because the study was not started or not completed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0103">
                            <items id="text">Unknown</items>
                            <items id="description">The status is not known by the radiology source system.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0104">
                            <items id="text">Report identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">Unique identifier for the imaging report assigned by the radiology service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">As one instance of this archetype is used per study, this identifier can be used to connect more than one imaging study within a single combined report. Equivalent to DiagnosticReport.identifier in FHIR. </items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0105">
                            <items id="text">Study description</items>
                            <items id="description">Radiology service-generated description or classification of the study.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Equivalent to ImagingStudy.description in FHIR and Study description (0008, 1030) in DICOM. Possible DICOM examples: MR BREAS, UNIT; NEURO^HEAD; or MR BREASTUNI UE.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0106">
                            <items id="text">Image details</items>
                            <items id="description">Link to full details of any imaging carried out during the study.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Typically referencing images used the DICOM enabled modalities, but this data field provides an alternative method to access the images. Equivalent to DiagnosticReport.imagingStudy in FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0107">
                            <items id="text">Stabilising appliance</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of a stabilising appliance in use.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: braces, casts, external fixation.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0108">
                            <items id="text">Position</items>
                            <items id="description">Position of the individual during the imaging examination.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: standing or lying.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.advance_care_directive.v2</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Advance care directive</items>
                            <items id="description">A framework to communicate the preferences of an individual for future medical treatment and care.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Item tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Status</items>
                            <items id="description">The status of the advance care directive.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the advance care directive status with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Type of directive</items>
                            <items id="description">The type of advance care directive.</items>
                            <items id="comment">A short text description of the nature of the advance care directive. Coding of the type of directive with a terminology is preferred, where possible. It is expected that this is largely localised to reflect local policy and legislation.

For example, in the Netherlands, advance care directive types include, but are not limited to, 'Treatment prohibition', 'Treatment prohibition with completion of Completed Life', 'Euthanasia request' and 'Declaration of life'.

In the UK, advance care directive types include 'Advance Decision', 'Advance Directive' and 'Advance Statement'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description of the overall advance care directive.</items>
                            <items id="comment">May be used to record a narrative overview of the complete advance care directive, which may or may not be supported by structured data. Details of specific structured findings can be included using CLUSTER archetypes in the 'Directive details' slot. This data element may be used to capture legacy data that is not available in a structured format.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Condition</items>
                            <items id="description">The conditions or situations in which the individual wishes the advance care directive to apply.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: dementia, brain injury, diseases of the central nervous system, and terminal illness. Coding with a terminology is preferred, where possible.

The advance care directive applies to all specified conditions if the individual can no longer make or communicate decisions about their medical treatment and is unlikely to regain the ability to make such decisions. Details of specific decisions that apply to different conditions or situations can be included using CLUSTER archetypes in the 'Directive details' slot.
</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Item tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Witness</items>
                            <items id="description">Personal details of a person who witnesses the completion of the advance care directive.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example, 'John Smith, Lawyer'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                            <items id="text">Mandate</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of any legislation or other authoritative guidance that apply.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example, 'In England and Wales, advance decisions are covered by the Mental Capacity Act.  Mandate: https://www.bma.org.uk/advice/employment/ethics/consent/consent-tool-kit/9-advance-decisions'. 

Or 'Jehovah's Witnesses believe that the Bible prohibits Christians from accepting blood transfusions. Mandate: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jehovah%27s_Witnesses_and_blood_transfusions'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0030">
                            <items id="text">Location</items>
                            <items id="description">Physical or digital location of the Advance care directive.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0038">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the advance care directive not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0044">
                            <items id="text">Present</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual has an advance care directive.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0045">
                            <items id="text">Absent</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual does not have an advance care directive.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0047">
                            <items id="text">Unknown</items>
                            <items id="description">It is not known whether the individual has an advance care directive.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0052">
                            <items id="text">Directive detail</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured details about the advance care directive decisions.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This SLOT should also be used to record details for specific conditions or as per national or other local requirements. For example, in the UK, there may be a specific statement about whether to actively prolong life but only during pregnancy.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0053">
                            <items id="text">Valid period start</items>
                            <items id="description">The date/time that marks the beginning of the valid period of time for this advance care directive.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0054">
                            <items id="text">Valid period end</items>
                            <items id="description">The date/time that marks the conclusion of the valid period of time for this advance care directive.</items>
                            <items id="comment">'Valid period end' may often overlap with 'Review due date'. However, they may need to be recorded separately in circumstances where a document has an extended period of validity but requires an interim review. That may be due to changed personal circumstances/events or local policy.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date when this advance directive record was last updated.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This may not be a formal review but e.g. a typo correction.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0056">
                            <items id="text">Review due date</items>
                            <items id="description">The date at which the advance care directive is due to be reviewed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">'Valid period end' may often overlap with 'Review due date'. However, they may need to be recorded separately in circumstances where a document has an extended period of validity but requires an interim review. That may be due to changed personal circumstances/events or local policy.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0058">
                            <items id="text">Directive location</items>
                            <items id="description">Information about the physical or digital location of the Advance care directive.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0059">
                            <items id="text">Copy holder</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of a person who has a copy of the Advance care directive.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example, 'John Smith, Lawyer'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0060">
                            <items id="text">Digital representation</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital document, image or video representing the Advance care directive.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0061">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to extend the model with local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements; or additional metadata to align with FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_bindings terminology="SNOMED-CT">
                            <items code="at0044">
                                <value>
                                    <terminology_id>
                                        <value>SNOMED-CT</value>
                                    </terminology_id>
                                    <code_string>[SNOMED-CT::410515003]</code_string>
                                </value>
                            </items>
                            <items code="at0045">

                                    </terminology_id>
                                    <code_string>[SNOMED-CT::261665006]</code_string>
                                </value>
                            </items>
                        </term_bindings>
