                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.advance_intervention_decisions.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Advance intervention decisions</items>
                            <items id="description">Anticipatory decisions about the overall intent of care and possible interventions (including treatments, activities, and diagnostic or therapeutic procedures), asserted by a clinician.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Intent of care</items>
                            <items id="description">The overall intent for future care and treatment for the individual.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The proposed value set is based on the MJA reference. 
Coding the intent of care with an external terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Curative</items>
                            <items id="description">The condition is curable; all appropriate life-prolonging treatment will be offered.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Restorative</items>
                            <items id="description">The condition is treatable with potential for restoration to the previous state of health or a prolonged remission; all appropriate life-prolonging treatment will be offered.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Supportive</items>
                            <items id="description">The condition is incurable and progressive; active treatment of the underlying disease may be appropriate for specific symptom relief and/or short term life expectancy gains; focused on comfort, quality of life and dignity. Also referred to as 'Palliative'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Terminal</items>
                            <items id="description">Life expectancy is short (hours or days); no active treatment of the underlying disease or treatment-related harm; focus on comfort, quality of life and dignity.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Unknown</items>
                            <items id="description">The intent of care decision is not known.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Decisions description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about all advance intervention decisions, including the CPR decision.</items>
                            <items id="comment">May be used to record a narrative overview of the advance intervention decision that reflect the specific circumstances for the individual. This includes any limitations, or conditional circumstances applicable, to interventions. This description may or may not be supported by structured data, recorded using the ‘Per intervention’ cluster. If both this narrative description and ‘Per intervention’ structured data is recorded, caution is required as there is a potential risk of divergence. This data element may be used to capture legacy data that is not available in a structured format.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">CPR decision</items>
                            <items id="description">Decision about the extent of cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) intervention appropriate for this individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Full CPR</items>
                            <items id="description">All appropriate cardiopulmonary resuscitation treatments should be attempted to save or prolong life.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Limited CPR</items>
                            <items id="description">Cardiopulmonary resuscitation treatments should be attempted, as specified in the 'Decisions description' narrative or as structured data in the 'Per intervention' cluster.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                            <items id="text">No CPR</items>
                            <items id="description">No cardiopulmonary resuscitation treatments should be attempted to save or prolong life. Also known as 'Do not resuscitate (DNR)'; 'Do not attempt resuscitation (DNAR)'; 'Not for resuscitation (NFR)' or similar.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Unknown</items>
                            <items id="description">The CPR decision is not known.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Per intervention</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of the decision about possible treatments or activities that may be life-saving, life-prolonging or cause undesirable side effects.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Intervention</items>
                            <items id="description">Name of the possible treatment, procedure or activity.</items>
                            <items id="comment">While this archetype is intended for all clinical decisions about possible future interventions, the proposed DV_CODED_TEXT value set for the 'Intervention' data element is deliberately not intended to be exhaustive. Instead it has been designed to be inclusive of most of the common life-saving treatments within an emergency context. The alternate choice of the DV_TEXT option can be used to add a local value set or bind to an alternative external value set within a template.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Chest compressions</items>
                            <items id="description">Technique used to manually pump blood around the body during a cardiac arrest.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Defibrillation</items>
                            <items id="description">Use of electric shock to reset the electrical state of the heart.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                            <items id="text">Intubation</items>
                            <items id="description">Placement of a tube into the trachea to maintain an open airway, including a tracheostomy.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                            <items id="text">Invasive ventilation</items>
                            <items id="description">Artificial ventilation where invasive means are used to assist or replace spontaneous breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                            <items id="text">Non-invasive ventilation</items>
                            <items id="description">Artificial ventilation where non-invasive means are used to assist or replace spontaneous breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Peripheral intravenous line</items>
                            <items id="description">Insertion of a peripheral intravenous line.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                            <items id="text">Central venous line or any arterial line</items>
                            <items id="description">Insertion of a central venous line or any arterial line.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                            <items id="text">Parenteral fluids</items>
                            <items id="description">Maintenance of hydration by means other than eating or drinking.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0024">
                            <items id="text">Parenteral or artificial nutrition</items>
                            <items id="description">Provision of nutrients by means other than eating or drinking.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Arrest medications</items>
                            <items id="description">Medications used during an emergency resuscitation to prolong life. For example: adrenaline, atropine, sodium bicarbonate, calcium, other vasoactive medications.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                            <items id="text">Circulatory regulation medications</items>
                            <items id="description">Medication that supports heart function, including but not limited to: vasopressors, inotropes and chronotropes.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                            <items id="text">Antibiotics, antiviral or antifungal agents</items>
                            <items id="description">Medications to treat infections.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0028">
                            <items id="text">Blood products</items>
                            <items id="description">Therapeutic substance prepared from human blood including, but not limited to: whole blood; blood components; and plasma derivatives.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0029">
                            <items id="text">Chest tube insertion</items>
                            <items id="description">Insertion of a tube into the pleural space.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0030">
                            <items id="text">Cardiac pacemaker</items>
                            <items id="description">Insertion of an artificial pacemaker to control the heart beat.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0031">
                            <items id="text">Dialysis</items>
                            <items id="description">Process of removing excess water, solutes, and toxins from the blood.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0032">
                            <items id="text">Transfer to hospital</items>
                            <items id="description">Transfer to hospital for assessment or admission.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0033">
                            <items id="text">Transfer to Intensive Care</items>
                            <items id="description">Transfer and admission to an intensive care or high dependency unit.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0034">
                            <items id="text">Decision</items>
                            <items id="description">Decision about the identified intervention.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0035">
                            <items id="text">Recommended</items>
                            <items id="description">The intervention is recommended.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0036">
                            <items id="text">Conditional recommendation</items>
                            <items id="description">The intervention is recommended only in specific circumstances, captured using the 'Preconditions' data element.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0037">
                            <items id="text">Not recommended</items>
                            <items id="description">The intervention is not recommended.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0038">
                            <items id="text">Unknown</items>
                            <items id="description">The intervention decision is not known.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0039">
                            <items id="text">Precondition</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the circumstance/s in which a 'Conditional recommendation' is applicable.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element is only relevant if the 'Conditional recommendation' option is selected in the 'Decision' data element. For example:' "Transfer to hospital is only applicable in the circumstances where the individual suffers a fracture and requires immobilization for pain relief.#</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0040">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the intervention decision, not captured in other fields.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the justification or rationale for the intervention decision.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0041">
                            <items id="text">Additional details</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about advance intervention decisions.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: people involved in the decision-making process, if it is not possible to use the reference model participations; or additional locally relevant considerations.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0042">
                            <items id="text">Patient awareness</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about awareness of the Advance intervention decisions by the individual.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the capacity of the patient to understand the decisions.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0043">
                            <items id="text">Carer awareness</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about awareness of the Advance intervention decisions by family, carers or legal proxy.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the individual has no family.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0044">
                            <items id="text">Overall comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about all advance intervention decisions, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0045">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0046">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date and/or time when the Advance intervention decisions were last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0047">
                            <items id="text">Valid period start</items>
                            <items id="description">The date/time that marks the beginning of the valid period of time for the Advance intervention decisions.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0048">
                            <items id="text">Valid period end</items>
                            <items id="description">The date/time that marks the conclusion of the valid period of time for the Advance intervention decisions.</items>
                            <items id="comment">'Valid period end' may often overlap with 'Review due date'. However, they may need to be recorded separately in circumstances where a document has an extended period of validity but requires an interim review. That may be due to changed personal circumstances/events or local policy.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0049">
                            <items id="text">Review due</items>
                            <items id="description">The date when review of the Advance intervention decisions are due.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0050">
                            <items id="text">Mandate</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of any legislation or other authoritative guidance that apply.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: an advance care directive.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0051">
                            <items id="text">Digital representation</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital document, image or video representing these Advance intervention decisions.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0052">
                            <items id="text">Document location</items>
                            <items id="description">Information about the physical or digital location of the advance intervention decisions record.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0053">
                            <items id="text">Location</items>
                            <items id="description">Physical or digital location of the Advance intervention decisions record.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0054">
                            <items id="text">Copy holder</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of a person who has a copy of the Advance intervention decisions record.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Comment: For example, 'John Smith, Lawyer'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to extend the model with local content or to align with other reference models or formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements; or additional metadata to align with FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0056">
                            <items id="text">Rationale</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the logic and justification for the 'Intent of care' decision.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                                    </terminology_id>
                                    <code_string>[SNOMED-CT::450476008]</code_string>
                                </value>
                            </items>
                        </term_bindings>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Procedure</items>
                            <items id="description">A clinical activity carried out for screening, investigative, diagnostic, curative, therapeutic, evaluative or palliative purposes.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Procedure name</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the procedure by name.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the specific procedure with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Procedure detail</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured information about the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use to capture detailed, structured information about anatomical location, method & technique, equipment used, devices implanted, results, findings etc.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Preparation</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure to be undertaken is planned.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the activity or care pathway step not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Complication</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about any complication arising from the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this data element to record simple terms or precoordinated complications. If the requirements for recording complication are more complex then use of a specific CLUSTER archetype within the 'Procedure detail' SLOT in this archetype is advised and this data element becomes redundant. Examples: Hematuria after a kidney biopsy, tissue irritation after insertion of intravenous catheter.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Procedure request sent</items>
                            <items id="description">Request for procedure sent.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Reason</items>
                            <items id="description">Reason that the activity or care pathway step for the identified procedure was carried out.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the reason for the cancellation or suspension of the procedure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0034">
                            <items id="text">X - Procedure planned</items>
                            <items id="description">This pathway step has been deprecated as it was incorrectly associated with 'initial' status - use the new 'Procedure planned' (at0004) pathway step  which is correctly associated with 'planned' status.</items>
                            <items id="comment">(Was: The procedure to be undertaken is planned.)</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0035">
                            <items id="text">X - Procedure request sent</items>
                            <items id="description">This pathway step has been deprecated as it was incorrectly associated with 'initial' status - use the new 'Procedure request sent' (at0007) pathway step  which is correctly associated with 'planned' status.</items>
                            <items id="comment">(Was: Request for procedure sent.)</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0036">
                            <items id="text">Procedure scheduled</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been scheduled.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0038">
                            <items id="text">On hold</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been postponed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0039">
                            <items id="text">Not done</items>
                            <items id="description">The planned procedure has been cancelled prior to commencement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0040">
                            <items id="text">Procedure suspended</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been suspended.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0041">
                            <items id="text">Stopped</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been aborted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0043">
                            <items id="text">Completed</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been performed and all associated clinical activities completed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0047">
                            <items id="text">Procedure performed</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure, or subprocedure in a multicomponent procedure, has been performed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0048">
                            <items id="text">Outcome</items>
                            <items id="description">Outcome of procedure performed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0049">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the procedure, as appropriate for the pathway step.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: description about the performance and findings from the the procedure, the aborted attempt or the cancellation of the procedure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0053">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0054">
                            <items id="text">Requestor order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">The local ID assigned to the order by the healthcare provider or organisation requesting the service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This is equivalent to Placer Order Number in HL7 v2 specifications.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                            <items id="text">Requestor</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the healthcare provider or organisation requesting the service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0056">
                            <items id="text">Receiver order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">The ID assigned to the order by the healthcare provider or organisation receiving the request for service. This is also referred to as Filler Order Identifier.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This is equivalent to Filler Order Number in HL7 v2 specifications.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0057">
                            <items id="text">Receiver</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the healthcare provider or organisation receiving the request for service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0058">
                            <items id="text">Urgency</items>
                            <items id="description">Urgency of the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0060">
                            <items id="text">Final end date/time</items>
                            <items id="description">The date and/or time when the entire procedure, or the last component of a multicomponent procedure, was finished.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Only for use in association with the 'Procedure performed' pathway step, and in situations where the procedure is repeated on multiple occasions before being completed or there are multiple components to the whole procedure. This may be the same as the RM time attribute for the 'Procedure completed' pathway step.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0061">
                            <items id="text">Total duration</items>
                            <items id="description">The total amount of time taken to complete the procedure, which may include time spent during the active phase of the procedure plus time during which the procedure was suspended.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Only for use in association with the 'Procedure completed' pathway steps.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Multimedia</items>
                            <items id="description">Mulitimedia representation of a performed procedure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0063">
                            <items id="text">Body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the body site for the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Occurrences for this data element are unbounded to allow for clinical scenarios such as removing multiple skin lesions in different places, but where all of the other attributes are identical. Use this data element to record simple terms or precoordinated anatomical locations. If the requirements for recording the anatomical location are determined at run-time by the application or require more complex modelling such as relative locations then use the CLUSTER.anatomical_location or CLUSTER.relative_location within the 'Procedure detail' SLOT in this archetype. If the anatomical location is included in the 'Procedure name' via precoordinated codes, this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0065">
                            <items id="text">Method</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of specific method or technique for the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this data element to record simple terms or a narrative description. If the requirements for recording the method require more complex modelling then this can be represented by additional archetypes within the 'Procedure detail' SLOT in this archetype. If the method is included in the 'Procedure name' via precoordinated codes, this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0066">
                            <items id="text">Scheduled date/time</items>
                            <items id="description">The date and/or time on which the procedure is intended to be performed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Only for use in association with the 'Procedure scheduled' pathway step.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0067">
                            <items id="text">Procedure type</items>
                            <items id="description">The type of procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This pragmatic data element may be used to support organisation within the user interface.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0068">
                            <items id="text">In progress</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure, or subprocedure in a multicomponent procedure, has been commenced.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0069">
                            <items id="text">Procedural difficulty</items>
                            <items id="description">Difficulties or issues encountered during performance of the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Examples: The patient was agitated, insufficient emptying of the stomach before gastroscopy, a tumour in the bile ducts made it impossible to pass the scope through.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0070">
                            <items id="text">Indication</items>
                            <items id="description">The clinical or process-related reason for the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the indication with a terminology is preferred, where possible. This data element allows multiple occurrences. For example: 'Failed bowel preparation' or 'Bowel cancer screening'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>
                    <children xsi:type="C_ARCHETYPE_ROOT">
                        <rm_type_name>EVALUATION</rm_type_name>
                        <occurrences>
                            <lower_included>true</lower_included>
                            <upper_included>true</upper_included>
                            <lower_unbounded>false</lower_unbounded>
                            <upper_unbounded>false</upper_unbounded>
                            <lower>0</lower>
                            <upper>1</upper>
                        </occurrences>
                        <node_id>at0000</node_id>
                        <attributes xsi:type="C_SINGLE_ATTRIBUTE">
                            <rm_attribute_name>data</rm_attribute_name>
                            <existence>
                                <lower_included>true</lower_included>
                                <upper_included>true</upper_included>
                                <lower_unbounded>false</lower_unbounded>
                                <upper_unbounded>false</upper_unbounded>
                                <lower>1</lower>
                                <upper>1</upper>

                        <archetype_id>

                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Absence of information</items>
                            <items id="description">Statement that specified health information is not available for inclusion in the health record or extract at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Absence statement</items>
                            <items id="description">Positive statement that no information is available.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: "No information available about adverse reactions"; No information available about problems or diagnoses"; "No information available about previous procedures performed"; or "No information available about medications used".</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date at which the absence was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Reason for absence</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the reason why there is no information available.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: patient is unconscious or refuses to provide information. Coding the reason with a terminology is desirable, if possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.exclusion_global.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Exclusion - global</items>
                            <items id="description">An overall statement of exclusion about all Problems/diagnoses, Family history, Medications, Procedures, Adverse reactions or other clinical items that are either not currently present, or have not been present in the past.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of procedures</items>
                            <items id="description">An overall statement of exclusion about all Problems/diagnoses, Family history, Medications, Procedures, Adverse reactions or other clinical items.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'No previous diagnoses', 'No known family history', 'No known history of adverse reactions', 'Never taken any medications' or 'No known operations or significant procedures'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of problems/diagnoses</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion of all problems or diagnoses at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of family history</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion of all significant health-related problems in relatives or family members of the individual at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of medication use</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about the use of all medications at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of procedures</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about all procedures at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of adverse reactions</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about all adverse reactions at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                                                <archetype_id>
                                                    <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1</value>
                                                </archetype_id>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                                    <items id="text">Medical device</items>
                                                    <items id="description">An instrument, apparatus, implant, material or similar, used in the provision of healthcare. In this context, a medical device includes a broad range of devices which act through a variety of physical, mechanical, thermal or similar means but specifically excludes devices which act through medicinal means such as pharmacological, metabolic or immunological methods.  The scope is inclusive of disposable devices as well as durable or persisting devices that require tracking, maintenance activities or regular calibration, recognising that each type of device has specific data recording requirements.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                                    <items id="text">Device name</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.
</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                                    <items id="text">Description</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Narrative description of the medical device.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                                    <items id="text">Type</items>
                                                    <items id="description">The category or kind of device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">Not applicable if a category is already recorded in 'Device name'.
 Example: if the 'Device' is named as a 'urinary catheter'; the 'Type' may be recorded as 'indwelling' or 'condom'.Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible. This may include use of GTIN or EAN numbers.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0004">
                                                    <items id="text">Manufacturer</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Name of manufacturer.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                                    <items id="text">Date of manufacture</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Date the device was manufactured.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0006">
                                                    <items id="text">Batch/Lot number</items>
                                                    <items id="description">The number assigned by the manufacturer which identifies a group of items manufactured at the same time, usually found on the label or packaging material.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                                    <items id="text">Date of expiry</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Date after which the device/product is no longer fit for use, usually found on the device itself or printed on the accompanying packaging.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">This date usually applies only to single use or disposable devices.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0008">
                                                    <items id="text">Comment</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Additional narrative about the device not captured in other fields.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0009">
                                                    <items id="text">Properties</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Further details about specific properties about the medical device.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0018">
                                                    <items id="text">Components</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Additional structured informations about identified components of the device.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0019">
                                                    <items id="text">Asset management</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Further details about management and maintenance of the device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">For example: Owner, Contact details, Location, Network address, Date due for replacement, Calibration details etc.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0020">
                                                    <items id="text">Serial number</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Number assigned by the manufacturer which can be found on the device, and should be specific to each device., its label, or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0021">
                                                    <items id="text">Unique device identifier (UDI)</items>
                                                    <items id="description">A numeric or alphanumeric string that is associated with this device within a given system.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">Often fixed to the device as a barcode.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0022">
                                                    <items id="text">Catalogue number</items>
                                                    <items id="description">The exact number assigned by the manufacturer, as it appears in the manufacturer's catalogue, device labeling, or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0023">
                                                    <items id="text">Model number</items>
                                                    <items id="description">The exact model number assigned by the manufacturer and found on the device label or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0024">
                                                    <items id="text">Other identifier</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Unspecified identifier, which can be further specified in a template or at run time.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">Coding of the name of the identifier with a coding system is desirable, if available.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0025">
                                                    <items id="text">Software version</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Identification of the version of software being used in the medical device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">When the medical device is an actual software application, record the version of the software using this data element. When the medical device has multiple software applications embedded within it, record each software component in a separate CLUSTER archetype within the Components SLOT - either as a nested instance of another CLUSTER.device archetype or using a CLUSTER archetype designed specifically for recording software details (but not yet available at time of this archetype development).</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0026">
                                                    <items id="text">Extension</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0027">
                                                    <items id="text">Multimedia</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Digital representation of the device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">For example: a technical diagram of a device, or a digital image.</items>

                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Device use statement</items>
                            <items id="description">An ongoing and persistent overview about medical devices that have been fitted or implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Status</items>
                            <items id="description">Assertion about the fitting or implanting of devices, as at the date 'Last updated'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Never</items>
                            <items id="description">The device type has never been fitted or implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Current</items>
                            <items id="description">The device type is currently fitted or implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Previous</items>
                            <items id="description">The device type has been fitted or implanted in the past.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Device name</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the specific device, by name.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Start date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date of fitting or implant of the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">End date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date when the device stopped being used or was removed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Structured detail</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured detail about the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                            <items id="text">Body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the body site where the device is fitted/implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Structured body site</items>
                            <items id="description">A structured anatomical location of the body site where the device is fitted/implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the use of the fitted device type.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date this summary was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                            <items id="text">Next review due</items>
                            <items id="description">Date on which this device should be reviewed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                            <items id="text">Device</items>
                            <items id="description">Name of the type of medical device.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Assisted hearing devices, eyeglasses, contact lens, dental braces, dentures, orthotics or artificial limbs.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Multimedia</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital image, video or diagram about the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                            <items id="text">Device details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about each device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                            <items id="text">URI to original data</items>
                            <items id="description">Link to the original data about the fitting or insertion.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Commonly, this will be an ACTION archetype that contains the details about the fitting or insertion of the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                        </term_definitions>

                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Height/Length</items>
                            <items id="description">Height, or body length, is measured from crown of head to sole of foot.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Height is measured with the individual in a standing position and body length in a recumbent position.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">history</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>

                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Simple</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Height/Length</items>
                            <items id="description">The length of the body from crown of head to sole of foot.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">List</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Device</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the device used to measure height or body length.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Position</items>
                            <items id="description">Position of individual when measured.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Standing</items>
                            <items id="description">Height is measured standing on both feet with weight distributed evenly, heels together and both buttocks and heels in contact with a vertical back board.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the measurement, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description of any issues or factors that may impact on the measurement.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: noting of amputation.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                            <items id="text">Lying</items>
                            <items id="description">Length is measured in a fully extended, recumbent position with the pelvis flat, legs extended and feet flexed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Birth</items>
                            <items id="description">Usually the first length measurement, recorded soon after birth. This event will only be used once per health record
.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="[in_i]">
                            <items id="text">in</items>
                            <items id="description">inch</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                                </value>
                            </items>
                        </term_bindings>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-OBSERVATION.respiration.v2</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Respiration</items>
                            <items id="description">The characteristics of spontaneous breathing by an individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">history</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>
                            <items id="description">Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">List</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Rate</items>
                            <items id="description">The frequency of spontaneous breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Regularity</items>
                            <items id="description">The regularity of spontaneous breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Regular</items>
                            <items id="description">The breathing pattern is regular.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Irregular</items>
                            <items id="description">The breathing pattern is not regular.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">Clinical interpretation</items>
                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description which represents the clinical meaning and significance of the respiration findings.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding with a terminology is preferred, where possible. For example: Normal breathing; Evidence of respiratory distress; Kussmaul's respiration; Cheyne-Stokes respiration; or Apnoeic episodes. Multiple statements are allowed. </items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Depth</items>
                            <items id="description">The depth of spontaneous breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Normal</items>
                            <items id="description">Normal depth of breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                            <items id="text">Shallow</items>
                            <items id="description">Shallow depth of breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                            <items id="text">Deep</items>
                            <items id="description">Deep breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                            <items id="text">List</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0024">
                            <items id="text">Clinical description</items>
                            <items id="description">A narrative description about the spontaneous breathing of the individual.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: noting respiratory distress, use of accessory muscles or intermittent apnoea; noting characteristics such as stridor, sighing, grunting, groaning, gasping. 
Conducting a full respiratory examination, then some of these findings might be more appropriately recorded as part of examination findings.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Variable</items>
                            <items id="description">Variable depth of breathing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0037">
                            <items id="text">Exertion</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about physical exertion being undertaken during the examination.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The individual's level of exertion during, or just prior to, the observation.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                            <items id="text">Inspired oxygen</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of the amount of oxygen being delivered to the individual at the time of observation.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Assumed values of 21% oxygen concentration, Fi02 of 0.21 and oxygen flow rate of 0 l/min or 0 ml/min.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0056">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of any issues or incidental factors that may impact on interpretation of the observation.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: level of anxiety, pain, feeding in infants, tracheostomy.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0057">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0058">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Presence</items>
                            <items id="description">Observation of spontaneous respiration.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element may be useful in extreme situations where a formal measurement of respiration rate is not possible. For example: a subject is unconscious and distant to the observer, such as trapped in a car or has fallen down a cliff. Presence of respiration can be implied if the 'Rate' >0 breaths/min.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0063">
                            <items id="text">Present</items>
                            <items id="description">Respiratory movements are observed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                            <items id="text">Not detected</items>
                            <items id="description">Respiratory movements are not detected on observation.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0065">
                            <items id="text">Body position</items>
                            <items id="description">The body position of the individual during the observation.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0066">
                            <items id="text">Standing/upright</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual was standing, walking or running.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0067">
                            <items id="text">Sitting</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual was sitting (for example, on a bed or chair).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0068">
                            <items id="text">Reclining</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual was reclining at an approximate angle of 45 degrees, with the legs elevated to the level of the pelvis.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0069">
                            <items id="text">Lying</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual was lying on their back.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0070">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the respirations, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0071">
                            <items id="text">Prone</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual was lying on their front.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                        <archetype_id>
