                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Pulse/Heart beat</items>
                            <items id="description">The rate and associated attributes for a pulse or heart beat.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">structure</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">history</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>
                            <items id="description">Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Rate</items>
                            <items id="description">The rate of the pulse or heart beat, measured in beats per minute.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Regularity</items>
                            <items id="description">Regularity of the pulse or heart beat.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Regular</items>
                            <items id="description">The pattern is regular.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Regularly Irregular</items>
                            <items id="description">The pattern is irregular in a regular pattern,. For example, a dropped beat once every 'n' beats.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Irregularly Irregular</items>
                            <items id="description">The pattern is irregular in a chaotic and unpredictable manner. For example, atrial fibrillation.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">List</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                            <items id="text">List</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Position</items>
                            <items id="description">The body position of the subject during the observation.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1000">
                            <items id="text">Lying</items>
                            <items id="description">The subject was lying flat.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1001">
                            <items id="text">Sitting</items>
                            <items id="description">The subject was sitting (for example on bed or chair).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1002">
                            <items id="text">Reclining</items>
                            <items id="description">The subject was reclining at an approximate angle of 45 degrees, with the legs elevated to the level of the pelvis.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1003">
                            <items id="text">Standing/upright</items>
                            <items id="description">The subject was standing, walking or running.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1005">
                            <items id="text">Presence</items>
                            <items id="description">Presence of a pulse or heart beat.</items>
                            <items id="comment">It can be implied that the pulse or heart beat is present if Rate >0 /min.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1013">
                            <items id="text">Device</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the device used to measure the pulse rate or heart rate.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1017">
                            <items id="text">Exertion</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about physical exertion being undertaken during the examination.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1018">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about any incidental factors that may affect interpretation of the physical findings.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example, presence of a pacemaker, level of anxiety; pain or fever etc.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1019">
                            <items id="text">Method</items>
                            <items id="description">Method used to observe the pulse or heart beat.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example, auscultation or electronic monitoring.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1022">
                            <items id="text">Clinical description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the pulse or heart beat.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1023">
                            <items id="text">Clinical interpretation</items>
                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description that represents the clinical meaning and significance of the pulse or heart beat findings, including the rhythm.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding with a terminology is preferred, where possible. For example: Bradycardia, Extrasystoles or Sinus rhythm. Multiple statements are allowed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1024">
                            <items id="text">Present</items>
                            <items id="description">A pulse or heart beat can be detected.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1025">
                            <items id="text">Not detected</items>
                            <items id="description">A pulse or heart beat cannot be detected.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1028">
                            <items id="text">Irregular</items>
                            <items id="description">The pattern is irregular.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1030">
                            <items id="text">Character</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the character of the pulse or heart beat.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding with a terminology is desired, where possible. For example: full, thready, bounding, slow rising, or collapsing. Multiple terms may be recorded.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1032">
                            <items id="text">Palpation</items>
                            <items id="description">The findings are observed by physical touch of the observer on the subject.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1033">
                            <items id="text">Auscultation</items>
                            <items id="description">The findings are observed with the assistance of a device, such as a stethoscope.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1034">
                            <items id="text">Automatic, non-invasive</items>
                            <items id="description">The findings are observed non-invasively using a device such as a pulse oximeter or a stethoscope.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1036">
                            <items id="text">Maximum</items>
                            <items id="description">Maximum pulse rate or heart rate observed during a period of exertion.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1037">
                            <items id="text">Body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Body site where the pulse or heart beat were observed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1038">
                            <items id="text">Radial Artery - Left</items>
                            <items id="description">The left radial artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1039">
                            <items id="text">Radial Artery - Right</items>
                            <items id="description">The right radial artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1040">
                            <items id="text">Heart</items>
                            <items id="description">The region of the heart.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1041">
                            <items id="text">Carotid Artery - Left</items>
                            <items id="description">The left carotid artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1042">
                            <items id="text">Carotid Artery - Right</items>
                            <items id="description">The right carotid artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1043">
                            <items id="text">Femoral Artery - Left</items>
                            <items id="description">The left femoral artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1044">
                            <items id="text">Femoral Artery - Right</items>
                            <items id="description">The right femoral artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1047">
                            <items id="text">Finger</items>
                            <items id="description">An unspecified finger.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1048">
                            <items id="text">Brachial artery - Left</items>
                            <items id="description">The left brachial artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1049">
                            <items id="text">Brachial artery - Right</items>
                            <items id="description">The right brachial artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1050">
                            <items id="text">Automatic, invasive</items>
                            <items id="description">The findings are observed invasively using a device such as an arterial catheter.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1051">
                            <items id="text">Ear lobe</items>
                            <items id="description">The lobe of an unspecified ear.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1054">
                            <items id="text">Toe</items>
                            <items id="description">An unspecified toe.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1055">
                            <items id="text">Irregular type</items>
                            <items id="description">More specific pattern of an irregular pulse or heart beat.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Selection of a value from this value set is only valid if 'Irregular' is selected from the 'Regularity' data element.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1056">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1057">
                            <items id="text">Clinical interpretation</items>
                            <items id="description">Generic label to allow for any or all statements about the pulse or heart beat.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1058">
                            <items id="text">Rhythm</items>
                            <items id="description">Specific conclusion about the rhythm of the pulse or heartbeat, drawn from a combination of the heart rate, pattern and other characteristics observed on examination.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1059">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the pulse or heart beat findings not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                        <archetype_id>

                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Body temperature</items>
                            <items id="description">A measurement of the body temperature, which is a surrogate for the core body temperature of the individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">History</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>
                            <items id="description">Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Temperature</items>
                            <items id="description">The measured temperature.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                            <items id="text">Protocol</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Location of measurement</items>
                            <items id="description">Simple description about the site of measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                            <items id="text">Mouth</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured within the mouth.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                            <items id="text">Ear canal</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured from within the external auditory canal.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0024">
                            <items id="text">Axilla</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured from the skin of the axilla with the arm positioned down by the side.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Rectum</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature measured within the rectum.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                            <items id="text">Nasopharynx</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured within the nasopharynx.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                            <items id="text">Urinary bladder</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured in the urinary bladder.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0028">
                            <items id="text">Intravascular</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured within the vascular system.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0029">
                            <items id="text">State</items>
                            <items id="description">State information about the patient.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0030">
                            <items id="text">Body exposure</items>
                            <items id="description">The degree of exposure of the individual at the time of measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0031">
                            <items id="text">Naked</items>
                            <items id="description">No clothing, bedding or covering.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0032">
                            <items id="text">Reduced clothing/bedding</items>
                            <items id="description">The person is covered by a lesser amount of clothing or bedding than deemed appropriate for the environmental circumstances.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0033">
                            <items id="text">Appropriate clothing/bedding</items>
                            <items id="description">The person is covered by an amount of clothing or bedding deemed appropriate for the environmental circumstances.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0034">
                            <items id="text">Increased clothing/bedding</items>
                            <items id="description">The person is covered by an increased amount of clothing or bedding than deemed appropriate for the environmental circumstances.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0041">
                            <items id="text">Description of thermal stress</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description of the conditions applied to the subject that might influence their measured body temperature.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0043">
                            <items id="text">Skin</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured from exposed skin.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0051">
                            <items id="text">Vagina</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured within the vagina.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0054">
                            <items id="text">Oesophagus</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperatue is measured within the oesophagus.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                            <items id="text">Inguinal skin crease</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured in the inguinal skin crease between the leg and abdominal wall.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0056">
                            <items id="text">Environmental conditions</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the environmental conditions at the time of temperature measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0057">
                            <items id="text">Exertion</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the exertion of the person at the time of temperature measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0059">
                            <items id="text">Device</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the device used to measure body temperature.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0060">
                            <items id="text">Temple</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured at the temple, over the superficial temporal artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0061">
                            <items id="text">Forehead</items>
                            <items id="description">Temperature is measured on the forehead.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to extend the model with local content or to align with other reference models or formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements; or additional metadata to align with FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0063">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional comment about the body temperature measurement not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                            <items id="text">Structured measurement location</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured details about the location of measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0065">
                            <items id="text">Day of menstrual cycle</items>
                            <items id="description">Current day of the menstrual cycle.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The first day of menstruation is considered to be Day 1 of each menstrual cycle.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0066">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional issues or factors that may impact on the measurement of body temperature, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="Cel">
                            <items id="text">°C</items>

                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="[degF]">
                            <items id="text">°F</items>
                            <items id="description">degrees Fahrenheit</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Head circumference</items>
                            <items id="description">The measurement of the longest distance around the head.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Event Series</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Head circumference</items>
                            <items id="description">The measurement of the longest distance around the head.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Device</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the device used for the measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the head circumference not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative descripiton of any issues or factors that may impact on the measurement.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'uncooperative child','caput & moulding post birth' and 'haematoma'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>
                            <items id="description">Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Birth</items>

                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">SpO₂</items>
                            <items id="description">The saturation of oxygen in the peripheral blood, measured via pulse oximetry.</items>
                            <items id="comment">SpO₂ is defined as the percentage of oxyhaemoglobin (HbO₂) to the total concentration of haemoglobin (HbO₂ + deoxyhaemoglobin) in peripheral blood.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">List</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">Sensor site</items>
                            <items id="description">The site of the measurement sensor.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Right index finger, left earlobe. Coding with a terminology is preferred, if possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Inspired oxygen</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of the amount of oxygen available to the subject at the time of observation.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Assumed values of 21% oxygen concentration, Fi0₂ of 0.21 and oxygen flow rate of 0 l/min or 0 ml/min.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Comment on and record other incidental factors that may be affect interpretation of the observation.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example, motion, pain, poor perfusion, infant feeding, peripheral hypothermia, sedation.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                            <items id="text">Oximetry device</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of the non-invasive oximetry device used.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0034">
                            <items id="text">Exertion</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about physical activity undertaken at the time of measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0036">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">A text comment about the pulse oximetry result.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0044">
                            <items id="text">SpOC</items>
                            <items id="description">The oxygen content of the peripheral blood, calculated based on pulse oximetry and pulse CO-oximetry.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0045">
                            <items id="text">SpCO</items>
                            <items id="description">The saturation of carboxyhaemoglobin in the peripheral blood, measured via pulse CO-oximetry.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0046">
                            <items id="text">SpMet</items>
                            <items id="description">The saturation of methaemoglobin in the peripheral blood, measured via pulse CO-oximetry.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0054">
                            <items id="text">Waveform</items>
                            <items id="description">A waveform reading associated with the oximetry measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0058">
                            <items id="text">Interpretation</items>
                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description which represents the clinical meaning and significance of the measurements.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding with a terminology is preferred, if possible. For example, normal oxygen saturation or hypoxaemia.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0059">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">e.g. Local hospital departmental infomation or additional metadata to align with HL7 or CDISC equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0060">
                            <items id="text">Multimedia image</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of a series of oximetry readings, other than waveforms, expressed as a multimedia image or series of images. Waveforms should be recorded using the Waveform slot and associated cluster archetype.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0061">
                            <items id="text">Pre/post-ductal</items>
                            <items id="description">Sensor site relative to the ductus arteriosus in neonates, to determine whether the blood supply to limb of the sensor site is pre- or post-ductal in cases of patent ductus arteriosus.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Pre-ductal</items>
                            <items id="description">The sensor site is pre-ductal.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0063">
                            <items id="text">Post-ductal</items>
                            <items id="description">The sensor site is post-ductal.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                            <items id="text">Indeterminate</items>
                            <items id="description">Unable to assess whether the sensor site is pre- or post-ductal.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                        <archetype_id>

                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Body Mass Index (BMI)</items>
                            <items id="description">Calculated measurement which compares a person's weight and height.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Body Mass Index is a calculated ratio describing how an individual's body weight relates to the weight that is regarded as normal, or desirable, for the individual's height.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">history</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>
                            <items id="description">Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Single</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Body mass index</items>
                            <items id="description">Index describing ratio of weight to height.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Method</items>
                            <items id="description">The method of entering the body mass index.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Automatic entry</items>
                            <items id="description">Body Mass Index calculated and entered automatically without user intervention.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Direct entry</items>
                            <items id="description">Body Mass Index calculated and entered directly by user.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Formula</items>
                            <items id="description">Formula used to derive the body mass index.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description of any issues or factors that may impact on the calculation.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the calculation was made using adjusted weight or height.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the calculation, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Clinical interpretation</items>
                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description that represents the clinical meaning and significance of the body mass index.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: underweight, normal, overweight or obese.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Local hospital departmental infomation or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="kg/m2">
                            <items id="text">kg/m²</items>
                            <items id="description">kg/m²</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-OBSERVATION.blood_pressure.v2</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Blood pressure</items>
                            <items id="description">The local measurement of arterial blood pressure which is a surrogate for arterial pressure in the systemic circulation.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Most commonly, use of the term 'blood pressure' refers to measurement of brachial artery pressure in the upper arm.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">History</items>
                            <items id="description">History Structural node.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">blood pressure</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Systolic</items>
                            <items id="description">Peak systemic arterial blood pressure  - measured in systolic or contraction phase of the heart cycle.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Diastolic</items>
                            <items id="description">Minimum systemic arterial blood pressure - measured in the diastolic or relaxation phase of the heart cycle.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Any event</items>
                            <items id="description">Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">state structure</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Position</items>
                            <items id="description">The position of the individual at the time of measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">List structure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Cuff size</items>
                            <items id="description">The size of the cuff used for blood pressure measurement.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Perloff D, Grim C, Flack J, Frohlich ED, Hill M, McDonald M, Morgenstern BZ. Human blood pressure determination by sphygmomanometry. Circulation 1993;88;2460-2470. </items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Location of measurement</items>
                            <items id="description">Simple body site where blood pressure was measured.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Adult Thigh</items>
                            <items id="description">A cuff used for an adult thigh.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Large Adult</items>
                            <items id="description">A cuff for adults with larger arms.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Adult</items>
                            <items id="description">A cuff that is standard for an adult.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Right arm</items>
                            <items id="description">The right arm of the person.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                            <items id="text">Left arm</items>
                            <items id="description">The left arm of the person.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                            <items id="text">Right thigh</items>
                            <items id="description">The right thigh of the person.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0028">
                            <items id="text">Left thigh</items>
                            <items id="description">The left thigh of the person.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0033">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the measurement, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1000">
                            <items id="text">Standing</items>
                            <items id="description">Standing at the time of blood pressure measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1001">
                            <items id="text">Sitting</items>
                            <items id="description">Sitting (for example on bed or chair) at the time of blood pressure measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1002">
                            <items id="text">Reclining</items>
                            <items id="description">Reclining at the time of blood pressure measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1003">
                            <items id="text">Lying</items>
                            <items id="description">Lying flat at the time of blood pressure measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1005">
                            <items id="text">Tilt</items>
                            <items id="description">The craniocaudal tilt of the surface on which the person is lying at the time of measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1006">
                            <items id="text">Mean arterial pressure</items>
                            <items id="description">The average arterial pressure that occurs over the entire course of the heart contraction and relaxation cycle.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1007">
                            <items id="text">Pulse pressure</items>
                            <items id="description">The difference between the systolic and diastolic pressure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1008">
                            <items id="text">Small Adult</items>
                            <items id="description">A cuff used for a small adult.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1009">
                            <items id="text">Paediatric/Child</items>
                            <items id="description">A cuff that is appropriate for a child or adult with a thin arm.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1010">
                            <items id="text">Diastolic endpoint</items>
                            <items id="description">Record which Korotkoff sound is used for determining diastolic pressure using auscultative method.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1011">
                            <items id="text">Phase IV</items>
                            <items id="description">The fourth Korotkoff sound is identified as an abrupt muffling of sounds.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1012">
                            <items id="text">Phase V</items>
                            <items id="description">The fifth Korotkoff sound is identified by absence of sounds as the cuff pressure drops below the diastolic blood pressure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1014">
                            <items id="text">Lying with tilt to left</items>
                            <items id="description">Lying flat with some lateral tilt, usually angled towards the left side.   Commonly required in the last trimester of pregnancy to relieve aortocaval compression.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1018">
                            <items id="text">Infant</items>
                            <items id="description">A cuff used for infants.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1019">
                            <items id="text">Neonatal</items>
                            <items id="description">A cuff used for a neonate, assuming cuff is the appropriate size for maturity and birthweight of the neonate.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1020">
                            <items id="text">Right wrist</items>
                            <items id="description">The right wrist of the individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1021">
                            <items id="text">Left wrist</items>
                            <items id="description">The left wrist of the individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1025">
                            <items id="text">Device</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about sphygmomanometer or other device used to measure the blood pressure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1026">
                            <items id="text">Right ankle</items>
                            <items id="description">The right ankle of the individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1030">
                            <items id="text">Exertion</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about physical activity undertaken at the time of blood pressure measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1031">
                            <items id="text">Left ankle</items>
                            <items id="description">The left ankle of the individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1032">
                            <items id="text">Finger</items>
                            <items id="description">A finger of the individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1035">
                            <items id="text">Method</items>
                            <items id="description">Method of measurement of blood pressure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1036">
                            <items id="text">Auscultation</items>
                            <items id="description">Method of measuring blood pressure externally, using a stethoscope and Korotkoff sounds.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1037">
                            <items id="text">Palpation</items>
                            <items id="description">Method of measuring blood pressure externally, using palpation (usually of the brachial or radial arteries).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1038">
                            <items id="text">Mean arterial pressure formula</items>
                            <items id="description">Formula used to calculate the Mean Arterial Pressure (if recorded in data).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1039">
                            <items id="text">Machine</items>
                            <items id="description">Method of measuring blood pressure externally, using a blood pressure machine.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1040">
                            <items id="text">Invasive</items>
                            <items id="description">Method of measuring blood pressure internally ie involving penetration of the skin and measuring inside blood vessels.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1042">
                            <items id="text">24 hour average</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimate of the average blood pressure over a 24 hour period.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1043">
                            <items id="text">Sleep status</items>
                            <items id="description">Sleep status - supports interpretation of 24 hour ambulatory blood pressure records.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1044">
                            <items id="text">Awake</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual is fully conscious.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1045">
                            <items id="text">Sleeping</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual is in the natural state of bodily rest.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1051">
                            <items id="text">Toe</items>
                            <items id="description">A toe of the individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1052">
                            <items id="text">Confounding factors</items>
                            <items id="description">Comment on and record other incidental factors that may be contributing to the blood pressure measurement.  For example, level of anxiety or 'white coat syndrome'; pain or fever; changes in atmospheric pressure etc.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1053">
                            <items id="text">Intra-arterial</items>
                            <items id="description">Invasive measurement via transducer access line within an artery.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1054">
                            <items id="text">Systolic pressure formula</items>
                            <items id="description">Formula used to calculate the systolic pressure from from mean arterial pressure (if recorded in data).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1055">
                            <items id="text">Diastolic pressure formula</items>
                            <items id="description">Formula used to calculate the diastolic pressure from mean arterial pressure (if recorded in data).</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1057">
                            <items id="text">Structured measurement location</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured anatomical location of where the measurement was taken.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1058">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Local hospital departmental infomation or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1059">
                            <items id="text">Clinical interpretation</items>
                            <items id="description">Single word, phrase or brief description that represents the clinical meaning and significance of the blood pressure measurement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at1056">
                            <items id="text">Dorsum of foot</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual's dorsum of the foot.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="mm[Hg]">
                            <items id="text">mmHg</items>
                            <items id="description">millimeters of mercury</items>
                        </term_definitions>
