                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.tobacco_smoking_summary.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Tobacco smoking summary</items>
                            <items id="description">Summary or persistent information about the tobacco smoking habits of an individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Current smoker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual is a current smoker of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Former smoker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual has previously smoked tobacco but is not a current smoker.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Never smoked</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual has never smoked any type of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Episode start date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date when this episode commenced.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Quit date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date when the individual last smoked the specified type of tobacco.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. Definitions for a 'Quit date' vary enormously and can be defined using the 'Quit data definition' data element in the Protocol section of this archetype. This date will be identical to the 'Episode end date' for the most recent episode. This date could be used by decision support guidance to determine if the  individual is at risk of relapse, for example in the first 12 months since quitting.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Regular smoking commenced</items>
                            <items id="description">The date or partial date when the individual first started frequent or regular, but usually non-daily, smoking of tobacco of any type.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. For example, this date could represent when the individual commenced smoking every Friday night or at parties. </items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Overall quit date</items>
                            <items id="description">The date when the individual last ceased using tobacco of any type.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. This date could be used by decision support guidance to determine if the  individual is at risk of relapse, for example in the first 12 months since quitting.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Pack years</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimate of the cumulative amount of tobacco smoked using the specified type of tobacco.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Commonly used in assessment of cigarette use. It is calculated by multiplying the number of packs of cigarettes smoked per day by the number of years the individual has smoked. One pack year equals 365 packs of cigarettes. Details about how to calculate pack years for other types of tobacco can be found at http://smokingpackyears.com. The definition of a pack can be recorded in the protocol of this archetype using the 'Pack definition' data element.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                            <items id="text">Overall comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about all tobacco smoking that has not been captured in other fields.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: stopped smoking or reduced amount on becoming pregnant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date this tobacco smoking summary was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                            <items id="text">Typical use (units)</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimate of number of units of the specified type of tobacco consumed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the number of 'sticks' or 'full pipes' per day or per week. This data element is redundant if a value is recorded for 'Typical use(mass)'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Number of quit attempts</items>
                            <items id="description">Total number of times the individual has attempted to stop smoking the specified type of tobacco within this episode.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                            <items id="text">Episode details</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the specified episode of tobacco smoking.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0029">
                            <items id="text">Per type</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about smoking activity for a specified type of smoked tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0030">
                            <items id="text">Pattern</items>
                            <items id="description">The typical pattern of smoking for the specified type of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0043">
                            <items id="text">Overall description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative summary about the individual's overall tobacco smoking pattern and history.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this data element to record a narrative description of the tobacco smoking habits for this individual or to incorporate unstructured tobacco smoking information from existing or legacy clinical systems into an archetyped format.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0052">
                            <items id="text">Status</items>
                            <items id="description">Statement about current smoking behaviour for the specified type of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0053">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative summary about smoking behaviour for the specified type of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0054">
                            <items id="text">Cigarettes</items>
                            <items id="description">Also known as manufactured cigarettes, 'factory made' cigarettes or 'tailor made' cigarettes. Processed tobacco, manufactured into cylinder made of paper or a substance that does not contain tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                            <items id="text">Hand-rolled cigarettes</items>
                            <items id="description">Also known as "rollies" or "roll-ups". Loose tobacco, hand rolled into a cylinder using cigarette papers.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0056">
                            <items id="text">Cigars</items>
                            <items id="description">Also known as "large cigar". Roll of tobacco wrapped within a leaf tobacco or in a substance that contains tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0057">
                            <items id="text">Pipe</items>
                            <items id="description">Loose tobacco placed inside a pipe bowl.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0059">
                            <items id="text">Former smoker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual has previously smoked the specified type of tobacco but is not a current smoker.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0061">
                            <items id="text">Current smoker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual is a current smoker of the specified type of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Waterpipe</items>
                            <items id="description">Also known as "hookah", "shisha", "narguileh" and "hubble-bubble". Tobacco, often flavoured, is burned then cooled through a basin of water and consumed through a hose and mouthpiece.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                            <items id="text">Per episode</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about a discrete period of smoking activity for the specified type of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0065">
                            <items id="text">Typical use (mass)</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimate of the weight of loose leaf tobacco smoked.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element will typically be used for pipes and hand-rolled cigarettes and is redundant if a value is recorded for 'Typical use (units)'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0066">
                            <items id="text">Cigarillos</items>
                            <items id="description">Also known as mini cigars. Short and narrow cigar.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0069">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about smoking of the specified type of tobacco, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0071">
                            <items id="text">Quit date definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for the 'Quit date' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0072">
                            <items id="text">Pack definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The definition of the size of pack used as part of the algorithm for calculating 'Pack years' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Definition may need to be specified per type. For example, the number of units in a pack used for cigarettes may be different to cigars; the mass of loose tobacco in a pack used for hand-rolled cigarettes or pipes.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0073">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0074">
                            <items id="text">Overall pack years</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimate of the cumulative amount for all types of tobacco smoked.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The definition of a pack can be recorded in the protocol of this archetype using the 'Pack definition' data element.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0075">
                            <items id="text">Current smoker definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for the 'Current smoker' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Definition may need to be specified per type. Current smoker definitions vary in different jurisdictions. For example: USA Centre for Disease Control refers to smoking during the past one month and New Zealand Ministry of Health uses 28 days.
</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0076">
                            <items id="text">Former smoker definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for the 'Former smoker' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Definition may need to be specified per type of tobacco. Former smoker definitions vary in different jurisdictions. For example: New Zealand Ministry of Health refers to not having smoked during the past 28 days.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0077">
                            <items id="text">Type details</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the specified type of tobacco smoking.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0078">
                            <items id="text">Bidis</items>
                            <items id="description">Also known as Beedis. Thin hand-rolled cigarettes filled with tobacco and wrapped in a leaf, often tied with colorful string at one or both ends. They can be flavoured or unflavoured.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0079">
                            <items id="text">Never smoked definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for the 'Never smoked' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Definition may need to be specified per type. For example, the definition may not be zero, but less than a specified amount (as units or mass) smoked during a specified time interval.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0080">
                            <items id="text">Daily smoking commenced</items>
                            <items id="description">The date or partial date when the individual first started daily smoking of tobacco of any type.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0081">
                            <items id="text">Episode label</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of an episode of smoking activity - either as a number in a sequence and/or a named event.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: '2' as the second episode within a sequence of episodes; or 'Pregnancy with twins' if describing the smoking activity during a health event such as during a specific pregnancy.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0082">
                            <items id="text">Episode end date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date when this episode ceased.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. This data field will be empty if the episode is current and ongoing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0083">
                            <items id="text">Daily</items>
                            <items id="description">Smoking the specified type of tobacco at least once every day.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0084">
                            <items id="text">Non-daily</items>
                            <items id="description">Not smoking the specified type of tobacco every day.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0085">
                            <items id="text">Quit attempt definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for a Quit attempt used to determine value for the 'Number of quit attempts' data element used in this archetype.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'stopped smoking for one day or longer with the intention of quitting'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0086">
                            <items id="text">Overall details</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the overall tobacco smoking behaviour.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0087">
                            <items id="text">Episode comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about tobacco smoking during the specified episode, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0088">
                            <items id="text">Kreteks</items>
                            <items id="description">Also known as clove cigarettes. Cigarettes that contain a mixture of tobacco, cloves and other additives.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0089">
                            <items id="text">Overall status</items>
                            <items id="description">Statement about current smoking behaviour for all types of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0091">
                            <items id="text">Never smoked</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual has never smoked the specified type of tobacco.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0093">
                            <items id="text">Overall years of smoking</items>
                            <items id="description">The cumulative number of years that the individual has smoked tobacco.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element does not take into account the amount of tobacco smoked. It may be used to calculate the 'Smoking index'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0094">
                            <items id="text">Smoking index</items>
                            <items id="description">An indication of the cumulative amount of tobacco smoking exposure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This parameter is similar to Pack Years but based on units of cigarettes, bidis etc smoked per day, rather than packs.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0095">
                            <items id="text">Type</items>
                            <items id="description">The type of tobacco smoked by the individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                                                                <lower>1</lower>

                                                            </precision>

                                                        </list>
                                                    </children>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.alcohol_consumption_summary.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Alcohol consumption summary</items>
                            <items id="description">Summary or persistent information about the typical alcohol consumption of an individual.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Current drinker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual is a current consumer of alcohol.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Former drinker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual has previously consumed alcohol but is not a current drinker.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Lifetime non-drinker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual has never consumed alcohol.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Episode start date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date when this episode commenced.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Quit date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date when the individual last consumed alcohol.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. Definitions for a 'Quit date' vary enormously and can be defined using the 'Quit data definition' data element in the Protocol section of this archetype. This date will be identical to the 'Episode end date' for the most recent episode. This date could be used by decision support guidance to determine if the individual is at risk of relapse, for example in the first 12 months since quitting.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Regular consumption commenced</items>
                            <items id="description">The date or partial date when the individual first started frequent or regular, but usually non-daily, consumption of alcohol.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element is recording when a regular pattern of drinking commenced, rather than the first ever taste of alcohol. The first taste could be at a very young age and then no drinking until mid teens. In this case it is the pattern in the mid teens that is clinically significant. To differentiate between patterns of drinking, that has to be recorded in other elements in this archetype, for example in the 'Per episode' section.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Overall quit date</items>
                            <items id="description">The date when the individual last ceased consuming alcohol of any type.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. This date could be used by decision support guidance to determine if the individual is at risk of relapse, for example in the first 12 months since quitting.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                            <items id="text">Overall comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about all alcohol consumption that has not been captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date this alcohol consumption summary was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                            <items id="text">Typical consumption (alcohol units)</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimate of number of alcohol units consumed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Number of quit attempts</items>
                            <items id="description">Total number of times the individual has attempted to stop consuming alcohol within this episode.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                            <items id="text">Episode details</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the specified episode of alcohol consumption.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0029">
                            <items id="text">Per type</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about consumption of a specified type of alcohol.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0030">
                            <items id="text">Pattern</items>
                            <items id="description">The typical pattern of consumption of alcohol.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The typical pattern of use can be made more granular by coding with a terminology or a local value set in a template.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0043">
                            <items id="text">Overall description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative summary about the individual's overall alcohol consumption pattern and history.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this data element to record a narrative description of alcohol drinking habits for this individual or to incorporate unstructured alcohol drinking information from existing or legacy clinical systems into an archetyped format.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0052">
                            <items id="text">Status</items>
                            <items id="description">Statement about current alcohol drinking behaviour.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0053">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative summary about alcohol consumption for the specified type of alcohol.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0061">
                            <items id="text">Current drinker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual consumed alcohol during this period.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                            <items id="text">Per episode</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about a discrete period of time with a consistent pattern of typical consumption.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0069">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about consumption of the specified type of alcohol, not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0071">
                            <items id="text">Quit date definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for the 'Quit date' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0073">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0075">
                            <items id="text">Current drinker definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for the 'Current drinker' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0076">
                            <items id="text">Former drinker definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for the 'Former drinker' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0077">
                            <items id="text">Type details</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the consumption of the specified alcohol.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0079">
                            <items id="text">Lifetime non-drinker definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for the 'Lifetime non-drinker' value in each of the 'Status' data elements used in this archetype.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0080">
                            <items id="text">Daily consumption commenced</items>
                            <items id="description">The date or partial date when the individual first started consuming alcohol on a daily basis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0081">
                            <items id="text">Episode label</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of an episode of alcohol consumption - either as a number in a sequence and/or a named event.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: '2' as the second episode within a sequence of episodes; 'Life-long'; or 'Pregnancy with twins' if describing the alcohol consumption during a health event such as during a specific pregnancy.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0082">
                            <items id="text">Episode end date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date when this episode ceased.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Can be a partial date, for example, only a year. This data field will be empty if the episode is current and ongoing.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0083">
                            <items id="text">Daily</items>
                            <items id="description">Consuming alcoholic beverage at least once every day.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0084">
                            <items id="text">Non-daily</items>
                            <items id="description">Not consuming alcoholic beverage every day.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0085">
                            <items id="text">Quit attempt definition</items>
                            <items id="description">The applied definition for a Quit attempt used to determine value for the 'Number of quit attempts' data element used in this archetype.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'stopped consuming alcohol for one day or longer with the intention of quitting'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0086">
                            <items id="text">Overall details</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured details about the overall alcohol consumption.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0087">
                            <items id="text">Episode comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about alcohol consumption during the specified episode, not captured in other fields.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: alcohol consumed as a hangover remedy eg 'hair of the dog'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0089">
                            <items id="text">Overall status</items>
                            <items id="description">Statement about current consumption for all types of alcohol.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0091">
                            <items id="text">Non-drinker</items>
                            <items id="description">Individual has not consumed alcohol during this episode.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0097">
                            <items id="text">Binge drinking frequency</items>
                            <items id="description">The individual's typical frequency of heavy drinking over a short period of time with the intent of becoming intoxicated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0104">
                            <items id="text">Alcohol unit definition (mass)</items>
                            <items id="description">Mass of alcohol defining a standard drink or alcohol unit as used in the 'Typical drinking (alcohol units)' element in this archetype.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0108">
                            <items id="text">Type</items>
                            <items id="description">The name of the specific type or grouping of alcohol.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use of the coded text option is preferrable when possible. Alternatively, it's possible to use a local valueset or terminologi to record specific types of alcoholic beverages. For example: red or white wine; brandy or whisky; normal or low alcohol beer; or denatured alcohol (methylated spirits) as a specific form of rectified spirits.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0110">
                            <items id="text">Alcohol free days</items>
                            <items id="description">The number of days where no alcohol was consumed in the specified period.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0111">
                            <items id="text">Typical consumption (alcohol units)</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimate of number of standard drinks of the specified type of alcohol consumed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The definition of an Alcohol unit can be recorded in the Protocol.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0112">
                            <items id="text">Episode description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative summary about the individual's overall pattern of alcohol consumption during the specified episode.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: details about binge drinking pattern.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0113">
                            <items id="text">Binge drinking description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the individual's typical pattern of binge drinking.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0114">
                            <items id="text">Date first intoxicated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date or partial date when the individual became intoxicated for the first time.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data point is not intended to record an accidental intoxication but to identify when a behaviour pattern of harmful consumption may have commenced.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0115">
                            <items id="text">Beer</items>
                            <items id="description">Fermented beverage made from grain mash.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0116">
                            <items id="text">Wine</items>
                            <items id="description">Fermented beverage made from grapes and sometimes other fruits.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0117">
                            <items id="text">Cider</items>
                            <items id="description">Fermented beverage made from any fruit juice.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0118">
                            <items id="text">Mead</items>
                            <items id="description">Fermented beverage made from honey, sometimes with various fruits spices, grains or hops.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0119">
                            <items id="text">Pulque</items>
                            <items id="description">Fermented beverage made from 'honey water" of cacti.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0120">
                            <items id="text">Spirits</items>
                            <items id="description">Fermented beverage made by a distillation process. Usually has an alcohol content >20%. Includes liquers, cocktails and rectified spirits.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0121">
                            <items id="text">Fortified wine</items>
                            <items id="description">Wine with added spirits.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                        <is_unique>false</is_unique>

                            <lower_unbounded>false</lower_unbounded>
                            <upper_unbounded>true</upper_unbounded>
                            <lower>1</lower>
                        </interval>
                    </cardinality>

                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan</items>
                            <items id="description">Plan or sequence of discrete activities developed to achieve a specified management goal or treatment outcome, carried out by health professionals and/or the patient.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Name</items>
                            <items id="description">Name of care plan.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Care plan Need Identified</items>
                            <items id="description">Need for a care plan has been identified.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Commenced</items>
                            <items id="description">Care plan activities commenced and in progress.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Developed</items>
                            <items id="description">Care plan components identified and documented.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Expired</items>
                            <items id="description">Care plan duration has passed the 'Expiry Date'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Completed</items>
                            <items id="description">All activities related to the care plan have been reconciled or completed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan ID</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of care plan.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Expiry Date</items>
                            <items id="description">Anticipated date beyond which the care plan can be deemed 'expired'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Scheduled</items>
                            <items id="description">Care plan has been scheduled.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Reviewed</items>
                            <items id="description">Care plan has been reviewed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of activity performed/enacted against the plan.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                            <items id="text">Reason</items>
                            <items id="description">Reason for activity  being performed /enacted against the plan.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0032">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Aborted</items>
                            <items id="description">Care plan has been aborted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0033">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Cancelled</items>
                            <items id="description">Care plan has been cancelled prior to commencement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0034">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Postponed</items>
                            <items id="description">Commencement of care plan has been temporarily postponed to a future date.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0035">
                            <items id="text">Care Plan Suspended</items>
                            <items id="description">Care plan is temporarily suspended but intended to resume at a later date.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.service_request.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Service request</items>
                            <items id="description">Request for a health-related service or activity to be delivered by a clinician, organisation or agency.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Current Activity</items>
                            <items id="description">Current Activity.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Requester order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">The local identifier assigned by the requesting clinical system.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Usually equivalent to the HL7 Placer Order Identifier.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Receiver order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">The local identifier assigned to the request by the clinician or organisation receiving the request for service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Usually equivalent to the HL7 Filler Order Identifier.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0040">
                            <items id="text">Service due</items>
                            <items id="description">The date/time or description about timing for provision of the requested service/s.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element allows for recording of the timing for the service/s, either as a date and time, an interval of date and time, or a text descriptor. In practice, clinicians will often think in terms of ordering services as relative or approximate timing, for example: 'next available', or  in '3 months'. As clinical systems need more exact parameters to operate on, this '3 months' will usually be converted to an exact date 3 months from the date of recording and stored using this data element. If complex timing or sequences of timings are required, use the CLUSTER.service_direction archetype within the 'Complex timing' SLOT and this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Reason for request</items>
                            <items id="description">The specific problem needing attention or the clinical question that requires investigation.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Reason for request' with an external terminology is desirable, if available. This data element allows multiple occurrences to enable the user to record a multiple responses, if required.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                            <items id="text">Reason description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the issue or clinical query that needs resolution.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0065">
                            <items id="text">Intent</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the intended outcome of the request.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Intent' with an external terminology is recommended, if available. This data element allows multiple occurrences to enable the user to record a multiple responses, if required.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0068">
                            <items id="text">Urgency</items>
                            <items id="description">Urgency of the request for service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Specific definitions of emergency and urgent will vary between clinical contexts, clinical systems and the nature of the request itself, so have not been defined in this archetype. More precise timing requirements should be specified using the 'Service due' data element or the CLUSTER.service_direction archetype within the 'Complex timing' SLOT.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0076">
                            <items id="text">Supplementary information</items>
                            <items id="description">Supplementary information will be following request.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Record as TRUE if additional information has been identified and will be forwarded when available. For example: pending test results.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0078">
                            <items id="text">Information description</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the supplementary information.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0112">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to extend the model with local content or to align with other reference models or formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements; or additional metadata to align with FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0116">
                            <items id="text">Patient requirements</items>
                            <items id="description">Language, transport or other personal requirements to support the patient's attendance or participation in provision of the service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0121">
                            <items id="text">Service name</items>
                            <items id="description">The name of the service/s requested.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Service name' with an external terminology is strongly recommended, if available. This data element allows multiple occurrences to enable the user to record a multiple services as part of a single request, if required.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0127">
                            <items id="text">Request status</items>
                            <items id="description">The status of the request for service as indicated by the requester.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Status is used to denote whether this is the initial request, or a follow-up request to change or provide supplementary information. Coding with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0128">
                            <items id="text">Distribution list</items>
                            <items id="description">Details of additional clinicians, organisations or agencies who require copies of any communication.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0132">
                            <items id="text">Specific details</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional detail about the service requested.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0135">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the service requested.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data point should be used to describe the named service in more detail, including how it should be delivered, patient concerns and issues that might be encountered in delivering the service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0136">
                            <items id="text">Emergency</items>
                            <items id="description">The request requires immediate attention.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0137">
                            <items id="text">Urgent</items>
                            <items id="description">The request requires prioritised attention.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0138">
                            <items id="text">Routine</items>
                            <items id="description">The request does not require prioritised scheduling.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0141">
                            <items id="text">Requester</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the clinician or organisation requesting the service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0142">
                            <items id="text">Receiver</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the clinician or organisation receiving the request for service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0148">
                            <items id="text">Service type</items>
                            <items id="description">Category of service requested.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Service type' with an external terminology is desirable, if available. If the 'Service name' was coded, it is possible for this data point to be derived from the code.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0149">
                            <items id="text">Supporting information</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital document, image, video or diagram supplied as additional information to support or inform the request.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0150">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the service request not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0151">
                            <items id="text">Complex timing</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about a complex service request requiring a sequence of timings.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'hourly vital signs observations for 4 hours, then 4 hourly for 20 hours' or 'every third Wednesday for 3 visits' or 'Day 14-16 of the menstrual cycle'. If complex timing or sequences of timings are required using the CLUSTER.service_direction archetype in this SLOT, the 'Service due' data element becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0152">
                            <items id="text">Clinical indication</items>
                            <items id="description">The symptom, sign or diagnosis that necessitates the requested service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Clinical indication' with an external terminology is recommended, if available.  This data element allows multiple occurrences to enable the user to record a multiple responses, if required.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0153">
                            <items id="text">Order detail</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional details and instructions about how the services are to be delivered.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Order detail' with an external terminology is desirable, if available. This data element allows multiple occurrences to enable the user to record a multiple responses, if required.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0154">
                            <items id="text">Clinical context</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative information about the individual and their situation, providing relevant context for the request.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0155">
                            <items id="text">Urgent contact</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the designated contact person or organisation and preferred mode of contact for urgent or emergency notifications related to this request.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: if the outcome of the request requires an urgent or emergency response by the requester.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0156">
                            <items id="text">Billing guidance</items>
                            <items id="description">A recommendation from the requester to the receiver about the method of payment for the service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Private'; 'Government insurance scheme'; or 'Private insurance'. </items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0157">
                            <items id="text">Responsible payer</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the individual or organisation who will assume responsibility for payment of the service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This data element will only be used if a known payer has been identified. For example: an individual, such as a parent on behalf of a child; or an organisation such as a workplace or government insurance scheme.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0158">
                            <items id="text">Eligibility guidance</items>
                            <items id="description">Advice from the requester to the receiver about the individual's eligibility for the requested service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>
                    <cardinality>
                        <is_ordered>false</is_ordered>
                        <is_unique>false</is_unique>

                            <lower_unbounded>false</lower_unbounded>
                            <upper_unbounded>true</upper_unbounded>
                            <lower>1</lower>
                        </interval>
                    </cardinality>
